title: No Security - Beware of the Vulnerabilities
body: UPDATE: I decided to update my review because I think people should be aware of the VUNERABILITIES of these cameras. I would never buy another Wyze camera again. I got them for home security but my cameras were easily hacked and someone gained control of all of my cameras and account (even though I added 2 factor authentication and a difficult password). I could literally watch someone in real time adjusting the picture quality which made them useless for live viewing, they turned the cameras on and off as they pleased, messed with the sound quality, and the worst part is they were likely using them to monitor me. I turned all the indoor cameras off, I left the outdoor cameras on, because I wanted to see what else they were doing. They got more sophisticated and started replacing footage with older footage and changed the timestamp on live footage. Also, be aware it is super easy to jam someone's wi-fi also rendering these useless. They would still record to the SD card, but since the loser hacker was deleting and replacing footage at their leisure it didn't really matter that I had a SD card. If you could merely use these without wi-fi, it might be okay (because then a hacker can't access you cameras), but Wyze doesn't make that easy.The worst part is Wyze support was of no help what-so-ever. All they kept telling me to do is add two-factor authentication. Well duh, I did that and someone is still controlling my cameras. I had to throw them all away and get a wired system that someone couldn't hack. It would be nice if they had REAL support.OLD REVIEW: There are some things I like about these cameras and several things I don't like. I have 4 of them. The price is right. You don't have to pay a monthly subscription price and they will stream to a cloud which provides two weeks of free storage, and then overwrites itself. The cloud is only for the short bursts of recording when the camera senses movement. You can pay for storage of longer clips when the camera senses movement. You can adjust the sensitivity so it isn't recording every time the wind blows, but if you reduce the sensitivity too much it won't turn on when a car is right in front of your home. You can also purchase an SD card for additional storage and the ability to review the entire footage, if needed. They are good quality for detail on footage less than 25 feet away, so perfect for someone coming up to your front/back door. They also have mic and a speaker so you can use them to talk with someone on the porch.There are some things I don't like. The camera lenses aren't good enough to identify a license plate or detailed facial features 30-50 feet away. They aren't weatherproof. Although I do have one outside under my eave that is somewhat protected from the elements and it has been okay so far. The night vision could be better. The camera outside has slightly better night vision, but it would still be difficult to make out detail of a persons facial features. If you have the night vision on behind glass you get a reflection. Also, I notice when it is recording movement sometimes the cameras freeze up, which is frustrating if there is critical footage you are trying to watch/review. When rewinding the SD card footage, it can be difficult to get to the exact time frame you are looking for, because the timeline to review footage is touchy and skips around or buffers and then goes back to the end of the footage. I wish they could come up with software that could take you to the exact time you are seeking. Also, every time they have updates, there are several issues and bugs. I wish they would thoroughly test the firmware and software updates before release. I am not going to update my cameras anymore if I have a stable version of the firmware and app because the new updates are riddled with problems. Their support team are of no help. They run you through all sorts of useless steps that don't help because it is really a firmware issue. Also, I wish they would build a feature into the app that you can easily go back to an older firmware version, if the update is buggy/has issues (which seems to be the case most of the time).Additionally, something to take note of is that an SD card can only be rewritten so many times before the media becomes too thin and it starts having errors. Mine lasted about 4 months, now I can't rewind footage, it just keeps skipping back to the end every time I try to review a specific time frame. Also, I wish they had a web based app because it can be difficult to review everything on your phone. You can save MP4s and email them, which is useful if you need to turn footage over to the police. The footage tends to be more clear on a computer screen than a phone screen.Overall, for the price, I feel these are okay cameras and they have helped me when I had to turn footage of a crime over to police. It's why I purchased 4 and have recommended the cameras to several others who have also purchased them. I wish WYZE could get their act together when it comes to thoroughly testing and vetting new firmware and software before release and making it more simple to go back to an older version, if there are bugs with the new versions.

title: **DO NOT Buy a Wyze Cam - Here’s Why**
body: **DO NOT Buy a Wyze Cam - Here’s Why**We have a total of 6 Wyze Cam V2 cameras purchased with a 4 year warranty, when purchased they offered free cloud events recording, 12 second events, 1 every 5 minutes. Now, the cameras are absolutely and completely useless without cloud, since you can’t fast forward through the timeline on your local MicroSD card recordings, so unless you KNOW the exact time an event occurred, forget finding it randomly, or accidentally coming across the event you are looking to locate.You cannot USE a Mac or PC to review the recordings unless you SWAP the Micro SD card out (which are a pain in the neck to unmount) and install a new Micro SD card, for 6 cameras you would have to spend hours daily, swapping Micro SD cards, downloading & reviewing Micro SD card video footage on a real computer screen.If; I pay for ‘Cam Plus’ Cloud Services it would cost (for a total of 6 cameras) $90.oo µ$ per year (billed annually) @ 1.25 per month (in order to make it sound like a small amount of cost.)If you simply buy a complete system. Example: $400 with a DVR or NVR recording device, you can get the alerts to your mobile device(s) absolutely free of charge indefinitely. Your video is 100% reviewable on a PC or Mac, Desktop, Laptop, Android, iPad, iPhone, etc. And in many cases, they have “popup screenshots” as you scan the timeline of your video with your mouse, so you can see exactly what is in the video without even watching the video first. Much faster and far more efficient than the Wyze Cam force you to buy/pay monthly (billed annually) Cam Plus cloud services.In 4 years of spending money with the Wyze Cam for Cam Plus, you could invest in a serious security system with DVR or NVR. Into your 5th year of Wyze you would be spending another $90.oo µ$ for Cam Plus cloud services.All of my cameras now say ‘Unknown Device’ when I attempt to access the events in the cloud. Meaning, I cannot access the 12 second events in the cloud free of charge anymore. When I purchased the cameras cloud was free indefinitely. With the limitation 12 second events, 1 every 5 minutes. Give me back what I originally purchased.I am completely dissatisfied with the purchase of these Wyze Cam’s some more than 4 years ago, some newer. If I had known all 6 of these cameras would be rendered completely useless by this new Wyze Cam Policy, I would never have purchased 6 Wyze Cam’s in the first place. IN fact I would never have purchased any Wyze Cam products at all. There is NO OTHER option except to pay for service I do not want in order to be able to properly monitor the 6 cameras. There is NO OPTION TO RECORD THE EVENTS TO MY OWN Micro SD card internally. WHY NOT? Other cameras offer this option.Wyze WILL NEVER make it possible for you (the end user) to CHOOSE to record events to YOUR OWN internal Micro SD card because that would render their cloud (Cam Plus), and thus their monthly revenue stream null and void. Why pay for something you can record and review yourself. That has always been the plan. Let me record my OWN events to the internal Micro SD card. End of story.I read 1 commenter: if you don't like it, then don't buy a Wyze Cam. This is counter productive and completely negative and serves no purpose.Well, that is a moot point at this stage. I already own 6 Wyze Cams (with extended 4 year warranty) and have for many years now. Besides, it is not the cameras I am dissatisfied with, it is the Wyze policy of money first, customer requested features & customer service second. Forcing potential customers to buy/purchase/pay for a service they do not want, is a business model which is destined to failure. Especially in light of the fact that other cameras offer the EXACT features discussed. Furthermore, it is completely absurd to treat the public as if they are idiots who will fall for such dishonest business practices & tactics. Mark my words this will come back to haunt you eventually once word gets out, and it eventually WILL get out.You apparently did not learn anything working at Amazon, the customer ALWAYS comes first, they are the ones who provide profit. Any other consideration is secondary. If it is not, your business model is guaranteed to fail.If you bought a Wyze Cam I recommend you SEND IT BACK as quickly as possible.Wyze Cam thinks people don't join this forum until AFTER they buy a Wyze Cam. This will be posted at other locations for this very reason. So we can catch the potential buyer BEFORE they make the mistake and waste their money. When you buy a Wyze Cam, it is conveniently hidden that cloud is a monthly cost and how much.I bought a Lorex Security Camera Package - Metal casings, Outdoor Cameras, Free monitoring/Alerts with an optional paid plan if you choose.Thank you,I may edit this as time permits

title: Undependable and constant hassle!
body: UPDATE: I waited for the second generation of these cameras, and tried them again. Initially it seemed like a home run! BUT.... when a micro SD card was installed in any of the 4 cameras I purchased, it would record until the card was full, and even though the setting were for it to erase/continue to record, ALL of the cameras with cards installed stopped recording, and rendered the micro SD cards unusable....that right....COMPLETELY UNUSABLE IN ANY DEVICE. In other words these cameras EAT MICRO SD CARDS. I tried different brands and capacities, including those branded as Wyze cards.....same result. So once again, I try to seek/get support from the company, and just like before, got no help or response to anything until I went on the public forum and started complaining about this issue. It wasn't until a large number of other customers joined in the thread and stated they were having the same problems. This dragged on for nearly a year, with Wyze doing everything to pin the blame on owners/users. Wyze even asked me to return ALL the cameras I had purchased...and when I asked for replacements, they told me that they would return my cameras when they were done investigating them.... and just how long will that take? They didn't know, and would not send replacements while they studied mine..... wow....what a screw job! After more and more people came forward on the forums, Wyze contacted me via email, and said that they were going to refund my money for all the cameras I owned, provided I would no longer say anything on the forums. In other words... "hush money". I agreed, and now here it is more then TWO YEARS later, and that thread is still very active on the Wyze forums..... and Wyze has never found or fixed the problem..... but they've had plenty of time create new products. OK, all that being said, the WyzeCam is a decent product, but it IS NOT AS ADVERTISED! It's great if you just want LIVE surveillance coverage ONLY, but it's useless if you wish to install and use recording media (micro SD card). Also, don't believe their "2 week cloud storage"..... because you can't get to, no matter how hard you try.I purchase two of these cameras directly from Wyze Labs, and for the price, expected SOME shortcomings..... what I got was products that are totally unreliable, requiring constant resets/re-connection to get any use at all out of them. Both cameras would constantly fail to connect to ANY device, making them useless to me. After 2 weeks of hassles, I tried to contact support, and waited..... nothing! So I went to the forum on their website and at first, attempted to get some help there. Based on many forum posts, these devices were obviously rushed to market, without enough testing of either hardware or software. People spoke of interference from any number of other devices, and one individual basically had to shut down ALL other electronics in his home to get a reliable connection! It was not until I openly started complaining on the forums, that Wyze finally responded to the support request I had submitted. They then wanted to play the twenty questions game......even though I had explained my level of electronics knowledge, and all the circumstances/steps involved in the initial support request. At that point it had been nearly a month since receiving the cameras, and submitting the support request. I finally told support that I was not interested in playing games, and to their credit, the issued me a refund. I really wanted to like these devices, but it was very obvious that they simply were rushed to market without enough testing of both hardware and software. Because they issued me a refund, I am open to trying the next generation of these devices, but will demand that they work as advertised. Some may say that it's only a $25 camera, so don't expect too much....but that is no excuse for a device that simply does not work as advertised.

title: Don't expect product support.  [Wyze Ticket 834534]
body: This is what you get with WYZE. Cams worked for a few months. I went from ecstatic to disgust.Written May 5, 2020I think the product is tremendous as far as value. It has a CO2 detector and smoke alarm. It has excellent night vision. The pictures are a very clear 1080P. It has a magnet in the base for easy mounting. It has two way audio. It is an indoor camera, but I have it mounted outdoors in a safe place using the magnet for easy mounting.I don't like that there is no windows app. I had to load an android emulator. First I tried Bluestacks and that failed to work at all. The latest version just was bad, and came up with a black screen, and it hung. Then I tried NoxPlayer, and that one worked, but sometimes the WYZE app appears upside down. NoxPlayer works but can be flaky at times. The point is, though I had a bit of a hassle getting it to work on my PC, but I did it, and it does work.It has motion detection and can record video or photos directly to a micro SD card on the camera itself. You can also use free cloud storage from WYZE to access the videoq or images from the internet if you so choose. You don't have to use the cloud at all. It can trigger on an event and has a person detection feature, and can notify you when it sees a person. It tracks motion as well putting a green rectangle around the person that is moving. It cna notify you when this type of event occurs.Overall, when you consider the low cost and huge capability, the hassles to get it working on a PC are worth it. If you are only going to use it from a smart phone, android or I-phone, you will not have a problem. And it integrates with Amazon Alexa if you have an echo show. So why wait? For this low cost everybody can afford a security camera.May 21, 2020I received my second camera. It is installed in my garage for CO2 and smoke detection as well as protecting my belongings in the garage.June 13, 2020Just ordered my third camera. I will use this one to watch my cars in the driveway and the street and neighborhood beyond. These cameras are top notch quality and I really feel much more secure having them and knowing what is happening to my property 24/7. I also bought two outdoor covers, one for the first camera on my patio and the other for my 3rd camera. This is so easy and so inexpensive, and it gives me peace of mind. These little cameras are fantastic!Sept 28, 2020Wyze does not respond to requests for product support at all. Zero, nada, zilch nothing. They don't care. They give a low price and that is the end of the conversation. I am a product backer of their new outdoor cam, and they would not give me the time of day when I had questions about it. In fact WYZE has never answered a single query I have made. They have the worst product support of any company I have ever dealt with.Oct 22, 2020Now I cannot get their WYZE Cam's to connect at all. I tried to reconnect a cam to the wifi and it no longer reconnects. Their support is dead silent. I am trying every day to get some kind of support from them. They offer no email, phone, or any other response other than a stupid robot page that tries to walk you through troubleshooting steps. I know how to setup their cameras, it just stopped working. I suspect it had to do with recent firmware upgrades. They are getting worse.Nov 16, 2020I actually was able to work with support. The cams still don't work. I downloaded an old firmware version from July and reverted on of the Cams to that version. This did not help. Support then had me run a program which sends diagnostic info to a server. You then send them the security key to unlock the diagnostic file. This was done on Nov 5th, and it's been 11 days since I have done that. So it looks like the dropped the ball.This is what you get with WYZE.

title: WYZE PRODUCTS NOT SECURE!  SPYBOTS from CHINA
body: The concept of inexpensive smart home products is great. Wyze has offerings now for outdoor cameras, robot vacuums, smart outlets, smart light bulbs, a smart hub, video doorbells, and more to come (smart controlled sprinkler system). The cameras themselves are cheaply made as to be expected from such inexpensive cameras, but the video quality is very good for the price paid. The problem I have is with the controlling app and the fact that all information gathered as well as videos goes back to servers in mainland China. There is no way to get around this and still access the cameras remotely. There are many other reviewers who expressed this concern before me. I did not do my research properly and now have cameras that I have to be careful in their use or sell to someone not concerned about privacy and security.I first became aware of the security problem when I installed the Wyze app on my android phone and attempted to sync it with the Wyzecam v2 camera. The app would sync and the camera showed it was connected to the internet but I could not get the real time video stream on my phone even when my phone was connected to the same wifi network as the camera. I tried everything that the troubleshooting guides asked like ensuring that mac filtering was turned off on the router (the requirement that mac filtering be off on the router was an important clue about how the Wyzecam app will force access to the internet and remote servers), restarting the camera, restarting the Wyzecam app on the phone, uninstalling and reinstalling the camera on the phone, uninstalling and reinstalling the Wyzecam app on the phone, reformatting the camera and reinstalling the latest firmware, rebooting the router and modem, and every other trick I found on the internet to get it to work. I only had success when I turned off the VPN on the phone. I immediately was able to access the video stream from the camera. I repeated this several times to verify that, when the phone was run through a VPN, I could not get my phone to sync to the camera to get video, audio, or event notifications. I tried a couple of VPN services and got the same results. I could only access the cameras using an insecure connection.There is about a 3 second delay from when the actual event occurs to when it shows up on the phone even when the camera and phone are co-located in the same room connected to the same wifi network and within 3 meters of the wireless router. This means that the video stream is going from the camera to the remote Wyze server (located in mainland China - PRC and/or Japan I later found out) and back to my cell phone. Even with a microSD card installed in the camera, the video stream still goes through the servers in mainland China and back to your phone.Other people have decided try another firmware (Dafang) that you have to flash to the camera then send the camera output to a DVR and use some other software to view. All this is possible but, at what point is it just easier to purchase reasonably priced POE cameras and connect them to a storage drive on a laptop/server/NAS and run something like Blue Iris software and get the images and remote viewing you want along with notifications and with real security?In conclusion, the Wyze smart home systems are really neat in concept and they do have attractive devices which will work for people who just want to use the product, not learn to program or code. The cost is giving up all expectations of privacy or security. Personally, I believe there are inexpensive cameras available which take a little more to set up but have the privacy and security I need.

title: Very Buggy with Numerous Firmware and Software Issues
body: NOPE, JUST NOPE! These cameras are not secure and are easily compromised. Mine were hacked and someone else was controlling them and using them to monitor me. After doing some digging I found out it isn't very difficult to do this, even if you have 2-factor authentication on. I couldn't get help from Wyze and was past the return period. I DO NOT RECOMMEND THESE CAMERAS.Previous Review: There are some things I like about these cameras. I have 4 of them. The price is right. You don't have to pay a monthly subscription price and they will stream to a cloud which provides two weeks of free storage, and then overwrites itself. The cloud is only for the short bursts of recording when the camera senses movement. You can also pay for storage of longer clips when the camera senses movement. You can adjust the sensitivity so it isn't recording every time the wind blows, but if you reduce the sensitivity too much it won't turn on when a car is right in front of your home. You can also purchase an SD card for additional storage and the ability to review the entire footage, if needed. They are good quality for detail on footage less than 25 feet away, so perfect for someone coming up to your front/back door. They also have mic and a speaker so you can use them to talk with someone on the porch.There are some things I don't like. The camera lenses aren't good enough to identify a license plate or detailed facial features 30-50 feet away. They aren't weatherproof. Although I do have one outside under my eave that is somewhat protected from the elements and it has been okay so far. The night vision could be better. The camera outside has slightly better night vision, but it would still be difficult to make out detail of a persons facial features. If you have the night vision on behind glass you get a reflection. Also, I notice when it is recording movement sometimes the cameras freeze up, which is frustrating if there is critical footage you are trying to watch/review. When rewinding the SD card footage, it can be difficult to get to the exact time frame you are looking for, because the timeline to review footage is touchy and skips around or buffers and then goes back to the end of the footage. I wish they could come up with software that could take you to the exact time you are seeking. Also, every time they have updates, there are several issues and bugs. I wish they would thoroughly test the firmware and software updates before release. I am not going to update my cameras anymore if I have a stable version of the firmware and app because the new updates are riddled with problems. Their support team are of no help. They run you through all sorts of useless steps that don't help because it is really a firmware issue. Also, I wish they would build a feature into the app that you can easily go back to an older firmware version, if the update is buggy/has issues (which seems to be the case most of the time).I also question how vulnerable these are to hacking. My cameras just shut off on their own and I have also had clips of footage completely missing even though I never turned off the camera.Overall, for the price, I feel these are okay cameras and they have helped me when I had to turn footage of a crime over to police. It's why I purchased 4 and have recommended the cameras to several others who have also purchased them. I wish WYZE could get their act together when it comes to thoroughly testing and vetting new firmware and software before release and making it more simple to go back to an older version, if there are bugs with the new versions.

title: Although, a great, compact, camera...  The new updates are super intrusive.
body: When this company started out, there was this big claim of privacy, affordable product, etc. All while, in a , Kickstarter, type or development.They definitely delivered on the feature rich product at a great price back then.Now though, still a feature rich product, you are bombarded by ads and always asked to share.This worked out great for a non-profit, military vets, that where going through hospice, that has a free room, food, but we had to do some., remote monitoring because of staffing. All at the beginning.. Fast forward to was this company demands today..Now, the app put out by this company, constantly asks to share this with them, and when you say ,no, you are directed to by the cam plus subscription.What happens if a worker mistakenly says yes? So, Wyze now has access to our vets in thier beds, coming out of the bath after showering? Where the hell is this info going to? Okay, they, either hate, or , do not care, about, something like this..Ohh... Microsoft, Apple, etc.... They collect collect everything, just like this app from Wyze, pushes, every single time, you use it. It is a sales cry to be bought out.They NEED bought out by one of these companies.Okay, not too bad, in itself. But. Okay, yeah, this is real bad.There is not one single way to turn these, telemetry gathering questions, off. I mean , just turn them off.I think the real people behind this project should be commended on what they have brought to market.At the same time, every new firmware and it's software update has brought more and more ads , questions, ...This is now a company looking for a buyout from a big player, (they can NOW show how much info they can grab). (But, hate to say, this company , has never lived or been to America)Wyze , now needs to be looked at as a , "Mid-Eastern", company, and as such, should not be trusted, by anyone. (Mid Eastern = China/Russia)I know, the , use case I have is really different than anyone else would use.But from we when invested, to now...There is no way we could ever, in good conscience, recommend this product or company. We have not one idea where to go from this point on.This company, hounds you for info ever step of the way. But, prices, are really good.Or, just go to, China, spec something out for all the Vet Clinics to ues, and , get rid of the middle man, like , Wyze, (Google, Ring, Alexa)..get something, we can , handle...We can not handle, this type of, software/hardware companies where I work.. We just do not have the , resources, the young people, that could help us.. We are presented, by thesez fake companies, that sell everything for, pennies on the dollar, to the governments that really hate the USANever listen to online sources like PCgamer or cnet to deliver any truths. They are considered, a, comic press. Paid reviews, for paid content. They will never let you respond to ANY content posted on any of these sites. They block every single way to respond..Find your own, listen and learn, and the NEW Wyze, can never be trusted

title: Seems to work ok, a good deal for the price
body: TLDR: Take a pass on this. It's a nice toy but the app is horrible, making this completely worthless as a security device.2/8/19:See below for my initial thoughts a few days after getting the cam. I'm updating and downgrading because the app stinks. It is completely unreliable. If you want a fun toy, get one of these. The video quality (tho not the audio) is decent. The IR is kinda cool for night vision. BUT the cam is absolutely, completely worthless for real security. Think about it - what is the single most important need for security devices? They MUST be 100% reliable 100% of the time. The Wyze app is anything but. Most significantly, the settings change randomly at random intervals. So I can set it to record and notify me of events, but that means nothing, because the settings will be changed once I leave that page. Now, for the last three days, I have received no notifications for any events at all. I have contacted Wyze support about this and have heard nothing back.1/7/19Just got and installed this a couple of days ago. Here are my initial thoughts:1) Setup is easy.2) HD quality video is pretty good (altho I'm not sure you could recognize someone who is 10 feet or more away from the camera, if you didn't know them).3) Sound quality is not great, at least at a distance - in video of people talking away from the camera I find it very hard to make out what they are saying.4) Motion and sound detection is very sensitive - too sensitive. I finally disabled the sound detection as it was picking up way too many false positives even with the sensitivity cranked down to 10% (whatever that means). At 50% motion sensitivity was picking up dust mites floating in near the lens. At 40% the false motion was reduced but not 0. Testing 30% now.5) Be aware that you cannot access your videos from a web browser. This seems like a HUGE oversight - a phone screen just isn't big enough to see much detail given the wide field of view for this camera.6) The phone app pretty intuitive and easy to learn all the settings.7) The 12 second limit for the clips that get stored in the cloud is pretty short. I'd like to see them increase this a bit and make it customizable. Of course they are giving cloud storage for free so I'm sure they have some limits there.8) The infrared is impressively good - no problem seeing in complete darkness.9) The auto mode (for switching between light vision and regular vision) works very well. There is a big of a lag switching out of infrared when the lights come on but the switch to IR when the lights go off is fast and effective.10) Given how short the cloud storage clips are, and given that you can only view them from your phone, having and SD card to record locally is pretty much required. I haven't tried to look at this yet from my PC but I assume it's in an open format so that is possible; if not shame on Wyze!11) I believe I have observed a bug ... you can turn the cam off from the app, but when I turn it back on, it turns itself off again. I have to turn it on a second time to get it to stay on. Not a huge deal but kind of annoying ... if you didn't notice this you could think the cam is running when it's not. I will need to remember to always verify it's on when I do this.Over all for the price this is a decent device - nothing amazing, but does the job at minimal cost.

title: Unstable platform, poor sound, poor picture makes this item hard to stand behind...
body: Cannot recommend.With all the listed specifications on paper, this seemed like a great buy on Black Friday as a $5.00 "add-on." It was a good chance to test newer technology without breaking the bank. Nowadays, there are so many different security products out there on the market, and so little opportunity to see them in actual use, that reviewing performance aspects of these things can get expensive.Unfortunately, I'm having a difficult time with this unit, and I'm not impressed. I'm not the most well-versed person in security camera technology. But I have installed a security system or 8 in my time. Not all of them were easy as some of them required fishing wires through internal/external walls (the earliest forms of security camera installations had no wireless aspects), but all of them worked in (in real time) the end. This one... not really.This unit works wirelessly. As such there are wireless limitations. Make sure that you have wireless coverage in the place you want this unit so it can work. Since it can be moved around, it is not difficult to test the actual capabilities of this unit (place near wireless router). Unfortunately, this unit has not overcome any wireless limitations, and continues to fail in nearly all aspects. There are still heavy "lag" times or delays when an actual incident is occurring and when you are actually able to view it. As such, audio aspects of this unit are also quite crippled (ever talk to someone with a 5 second lag? It will drive you NUTS). Furthermore, the low-light aspect of this unit is nearly non-existent or at the most, at the most minimum of standards. 5 feet away from baby's crib, and everything is hazy, blurry at best. Audio is static filled and unclear. No one at the other end can tell what I am trying to say through its tinny speakers. I can't tell what anyone is trying to say through its insignificant microphone. Is it my fiberoptic based wifi network being overloaded? Maybe? But doubtful. Why didn't I place it 2 feet away from baby's crib for low light? Because this item still needs a plug to power it and baby can reach most things at around 2 feet away. For those of you without babies, parents generally try to keep cords, strings (even with balloons on them) and strangley things away from baby at night... you know, so they don't strangle themselves... But don't think you can't do it (get some Gaffer's Tape - I was just too lazy). Also, if you were not using it for baby, but still using it for the dark, just remember that this unit's own, insignificant "IR" lights won't illuminate anything clearly over 5 feet away.-5 stars for coming in substandard on every level so far.+1 star for being able to connect with Alexa! Yay! Although it's own app will keep asking you to re-input the password to access the video each time you open it on your tablet or other such device... which kind of hurts.All in all, if it worked, it would be a pretty cool device. It is a bit and clunky (significantly smaller than a RING camera - but then again, the RING cameras actually work to some degree).The technology seems to need a bit more development, so I cannot recommend this item at this time. Maybe when the technology advances to its next generation, but not now.Thanks for reading my review! I hope this helps.

title: Latest firmware upgrade is a failure.
body: Update (3/27/21) since last review:The latest firmware (4.9.6.241) has really made my cam V2 pretty useless. At first I thought it was just with the sound detection, but now it is obvious it is alwo with motion detection, and especially with the AI (in Wyze that stands for Anti-Intelligence) detection, which is something you have to pay a monthly fee (Cam Plus) to have.To back up my claim, here are statistics over the last couple of days and I'm only including day light hours, so lighting is not an issue:Date Motion Events AI Vehicle AI Person # of AI notifications received3/25 20 (all vehicle) 1 N/A 03/26 20 (1 was person) 3 1 03/27 4 so far (all vehicle) 1 N/A 0One missed motion alert today (3/27) was the USPS mail vehicle delivering mail. I personally watched the vehicle and yet the Wyze (false advertising) Cam V2 did not detect, let alone the Anti-Intelligence properly process it. Maybe this is due to their AI being processed in China, so maybe they are required to block US government vehicles and people, which USPS would fall under. But wait, it actually detected the USPS vehicle back on 3/24 and it has no problems picking up FedEx and UPS, which I know are not US government vehicles. I do have to admit that I had the same issue with a Ring doorbell. Even had Amazon deliveries completely missed which required the delivery person to walk right up to the door. Do a Google search about motion detection cameras not picking up deliveries (USPS, Amazon, FedEx, UPS) and there are a lot of articles.Being able to be notified of mail and other deliveries is one of the main reasons that I moved the camera from within the garage when I ended up returning the Ring doorbell for failure to do such. Lately we have had mail delivery issues and I was hoping this would allow me to know if the mail was actually delivered and hopefully not stolen. But once again a company is only interested in getting your money, but not actually delivering anything for it. Is there a company left in the world that actually provides a good product or service?Wyze released an update to the App on Mar 22nd, which I just updated to today. It has made no difference, but basically they just added support for what I can only imagine are more worthless products.I've submitted two "logs" for the issues I've encountered after the firmware upgrade and nothing but silence. Like a moron I purchased the yearly subscription to "Cam Plus" as it came out to just over $1/month. I definitely will not be renewing the subscription when this runs out as it's not even worth the dollar. I personally believe these products should be listed under toys.Older reviews:I've been using since Nov 2020 with no real issues. But now with latest firmware upgrade 4.9.6.241 has made the device useless for my needs as no longer able to control which notifications I receive. I don't want sound event notifications and have them disabled. But after upgrading a couple days ago and having thunderstorms today I am getting notifications with every clap of thunder. Camera is in garage so not motion event.Good thing these are cheap so I can just throw it away.

title: You Get What You Pay For: Unreliable Security
body: The Wyzecam v2 is wholly unreliable security device. I don't understand the fervor of blind loyalty for these problematic toys. It's as if people tolerate this atrocious product in some weirdish affinity to support a young tech company. This product epitomizes caveat emptor.I gave the Wyzecam v2 an earnest try. I genuinely wanted the thing to surprise me as a feature-and-functionally-competitive, inexpensive alternative to established, ubiquitous IP security camera brands on the market. I knew better, of course. But, like all human-types, hope, however we choose to repress the emotion, is innate and eternal. I hoped it'd meet my expectations by... you know... working.Nope.The Wyzecam v2 is horrible. I found it a lousy product for many, if not all, the reasons familiar to folks who post their chronic problems here on Amazon, within the comments sections of various tech review sites, and within the forum of wyzecam.com proper. Peruse the usual--and unresolved--complaints; e.g: WiFi connectivity drop-outs; random loss of camera configuration; chronic "clicking" noise; screwy IR settings; firmware updates that break more than they fix; wonky SD card support; zero browser support; chronic app crashing... and on, and on, and on..So what's going on with Wyze Labs? Simply, the folks brought a functionally marginal (marginally functional?) product to market. This is a common strategy that vexes small undercapitalized startups. VC funding is conditional; investments must show profitability and/or deliver product within a strict time frame. Developers are under pressure to bring product to market that is ostensibly still beta; resources are rehomed on developing the next product destined for market, under similar--and unrelenting--pressure. Under this all-to-common model, there is simply too little resource remaining to investigate the inherent and emergent problems within existing product(s) AND develop, test, then avail updates. As for developing new features for released product..? That gets down-prioritized even further. There simply aren't the resources.Engineers espouse the truism that complexity is inversely proportional to reliability. I reason that the mathematical imbalance of $20 per camera imparts unreliability as well.I'm bailing on Wyzecam. My evaluation is over. Sure I'm out $20; but I'm much more grouchy about the loss of my time. But $20..? ..seriously? Set your expectations for the Wyzecam v2 as low as your security budget and you'll LOVE this product.I'll buy a reliable, industry-vetted IP security camera product that features secure local storage (iow, an NVR... which the Wyzecam v2 also lacks but cheekily touts as a feature), excellent tolerance to indoor environment, and the rock-solid performance one expects from a security device. It sucks that the cost of entry for this level of reliability starts at $500. But that, amigos, is the minimum cost of home security.And....cue soft piano music... isn't the safety of your family and home worth it...

title: PLEASE look at the bad reviews, the experience outweighs the "good" reviews!
body: Okay, I had to hop on my computer to do this review, no way I was going to type all of this on my phone. So, let's begin by saying if I can give this rating no stars or maybe even negative stars I would, the boxes are more high quality than the product itself and here it is the date I received it on my door step (10/11/19) I am sending it back and writing a review on it. Let me tell you why. I am looking for some cameras to put in my apartment unit because me and the wife have noticed things being out of place when coming home from work, so you know us loyal amazon shoppers that's the first place I visited. So, I came on here and stumbled upon this product and seen the questionably, concerning, mysteriously high amount of stars so I proceed to dig into why they have such good reviews. I read a lot of reviews and by a lot I mean maybe close to an hour worth of reviews and even the more lengthier reviews because if you're anything like me you really care about what you buy, where your money is going and how quality the product you are spending the money is. I read the good and the bad, and with the bad I wish I would have listened. Those bad reviews are what you will get IF you are very faithful like I was with this product. It is indeed true that the camera never has connection and what I really like about this is that the app tries to make it seem like it's your phone or your internet that's having the problem but it's not and I know it's not because I have 5G WiFi in my apartment and it works fantastic. How did I know it was working at the time of the camera not working? Because I went to youtube on my phone and watched an ad and the video I had on with no buffering included. Then in order to get the camera back to being connected to watch the feed, you have to power cycle the darn thing and hope and bet that it works then and we shouldn't have to do that. And if you don't have connection then you can't see the live feed obviously and the camera is not recording, so you get no recordings of what's going on in your area. So, I stepped out on a huge leap of faith because of a lot of reasons, however the main ones are maybe those people had bad experiences because they were reviews a bit earlier than me buying them, WYZE made a youtube update video on them doing a huge innovation in improving the product, which I like how the problem they stated customers were having complaints on was that customers were having too many complaints and didn't point out the issues about the camera not staying connected. Like I get it. It's 20 something bucks and that's what WYZE is sticking to. But I am a person of quality I am willing to pay extra for quality and RELIABILITY and that is not what WYZE has. So, please do yourself another favor as I'm sure you did at the least once in your lifetime and do not purchase this product. Do not be hard-headed like the ones that gave a chance for it to be messed up in high disappointment!

title: WYZE ADMITS DATA LOSS - are they spying and eavesdropping too?
body: Many of these responses, reviews and answers to questions about Wyze products read as though they were written by amateur novelists and online magazine authors. It is no surprise that various metareview analyzers rate the accuracy of the reviews "C" to low, meaning there are a lot of fake reviews. A LOT of them. Read through them yourself, and look at the profiles supporting them.I like the Wyze cams, they are functional, cheap, and Wyze claims to be geographically in the US, but I would never put their products in a home I live in, or in a business critical application. They are useful only for remote locations that require a superficial amount of observation. You can safely assume that access to them is absolutely NOT private, and to date, it doesn't look like any third party software can view them without accessing the Wyze cloud. These cameras offer no RTSP, ONVIF or other standard network protocols, only their cloud, which they admit they outsource to a Chinese company.There was a data breach reported in December 2019, in which a tremendous amount of personal data was exposed, data Wyze had apparently accumulated on over 2 million customers. The data was left in an unsecured database, so you can count on things like your GPS coordinates, WiFi SSID, and "body metric" data such as height, weight, gender and bone density. The security company that discovered the breach described it as the biggest such hack they had seen in their ten years, and said:"If this was intentional espionage or gross negligence, it remains a malicious action that must be answered in the form of a decisive, external, and fast investigation by US authorities."Wyze admitted that it happened, and said the number of people losing body metric data was lower. Fine, but was it actually an accident, or did they get caught? Are Wyze subsidizing the cost of these little cameras by spying and selling your personal data?Just do a google search for."Wyze leaked personal data of 2.4 million users" for more details.From the Wyzecam forum: "They say they utilize a 3rd party called ThroughTek (offices in china and japan - none in US) to handle the transfer of videos. Wyze Cam claims the content is sent securely to aws. Examining the network traffic from camera shows that content is routed thru many providers including AWS, Linode (US) Vultr/Choopa (europe) and Aliyum/Alibaba (china). They seem to also utilize NTP server in Russia."These cameras do work well for the price, but you do not get any sort of privacy. Someone is watching, and, probably, many people using internet camera scrapers are watching, too. And hackers (and maybe Wyze themselves) are profiting from the private data of yours that is now in the hands of anyone who wanted it.In short, these cameras work well, their technology is solid. But don't put one in your house.

title: I have 5 of these
body: I am, once again, updating my review. LISTEN UP. I have 5 of these. The hardware is excellent for the price. BUT THE SOFTWARE ABSOLUTELY IS CRAP. You will not be able to see live, record, playback or anything without serious software malfuntions. You have to do everything at least twice, maybe three times to get anything done. The cameras keep quitting in the middle of viewing, playing back or recording. Messes up everything and just takes so much more time to do anything, not to mention that you end up with a bunch of tiny length recordings that you then have to edit or stitch together via an external software program you have to by. NO, the free editing junk is just that. The app is just horrible. Even worse since the latest "upgrade". It only works on phones, tying up your device completely. You cannot do anything else while using the Wyze app. Nothing. It shuts down, stopping whatever you are doing if you answer a call, make a call, send or receive a text, etc. So don't plan on using your phone for anything. No multitasking allowed. My earlier review stands. This is an even worse review because the issues just keep multiplying. I have no idea why I bought a 5th cam.. That's the definition of stupidity. Guess I was hoping the manufacturer would read these things and reach out to try to fix them. I cannot be the only one with issues like this. But I do use the app a lot even though it adds hours to my week to try to capture the things I need. I just cannot afford the higher priced cameras, but maybe I am stupid and should begin to replace the Wyze one at a time.I am updating my review. I now have 4 of these, one of which is a pan and scan model. The cameras themselves work fine. However, the Wyze app is very weak. It will cause lots of frustration since the cameras are no good without the app.1. The app is only available for Apple or Android use. Even though the descriptions claims it works with Alexa, it cannot be loaded onto an Amazon Fire tablet. No computers either since they are either Windows or IOS.2. The app kept crashing before the latest update in January 2019. Now it crashes MORE. In order to know what happened, when you go to alerts the new version crashes more than half the time. When you go to record an event, it crashes more than half the time, often during recording messing up the recording. Better have good editing software because you are going to need it to stitch partial recording together. Since I post a lot to Youtube, this is a time consuming chore. This would be more useful as an app if it could play on my laptop.3. The new version of the app no longer allows moving from looking at events to looking at live stream. You now have to go back to the home page in order to live stream. Royal pain.Overall, the hardware works. These folks would do well to hire some real programmers.

title: ONE CAM DEFECTIVE OVERHEATING & BURNED OUT TODAY! GREAT THINGS COME IN SMALL PACKAGES! but......🙄
body: UPDATE #2-WORSE than 1 star as the bedroom camera continues to loose the network and after several attempts AND OVERHEATING it is damaged and refuses to connect. I’ve reached out several times with no success. I want a replacement as I purchase EIGHT cams in total FOUR FOR ME AND FOUR AS GOFTS FOR NEIGHBORS!! UPDATE #1- 5 stars for product BUT 1 Star forIntegrity because they reached out via email asking for a review and as a special gift for doing so, I could pick one of 3 items. I chose a multi card reader and have been waiting since March for my gift YET I have purchased EIGHT more of these great WYZE CAMS FOR FRIENDS A D NEIGHBORS. Also, I reached out to them 3-4 times asking what happened to my gift for review and no one responded except initial email asking to copy the review into an email response (I did) and gift never came.Original Review: Honestly, this purchase made my week!! I’ve been reading reviews and walking past the systems at Costco and BJS for a year or so but after this past week of reading reviews here along with the cost size and ease of set up, I one clicked bought it! It arrived the next day and last night I set it up. It took extra time because my WiFi password is so complex that I timed out during set up a few time. The teeny tiny doll sized instructions are perfectly succinct and appropriate to the small in exterior size yet BIG on quality and function. The above pics are 3 screen shots of a video it took for me of motion in front and when you watch the video, it places a laser thin green box around movement - dogs ducks owners etc. I have alerts set to notify me of movements and sounds so I receive a text but I have that to get to know the unit and it’s functions and behaviors. It truly seems somewhat amusing when it arrived because it truly is minuscule but performs as a high functioning covert agent. I plan on getting 3 more for back picture window and lake bedroom windows. It truly wowed me. I haven’t seen anything regarding facial recognition and the 180 choice in advanced settings had to be unchecked for me due to upside down live stream. The night vision choice also in advanced settings was at auto but it’s unchecked and the night vision and video was much better when u checked but I’m exhausted and may have not understood everything except that it’s working great and I’m so happy. I did have a bit of trouble getting the micro disk to stay and click in the slot and I realized that I felt I was on a scavenger hunt looking for the button for setting up the device externally and the card slot because they were so small and on a very small almost like a key chain sized Rubix cube. I would definitely recommend this for a quick and inexpensive security set up. I finally was successful formatting the disk when I turn the device upside down. It worked 👍🏼

title: Fails to catch any useful footage. Poor reliability.  No computer app.
body: Wow, great price and free online storage. Sold! I bought 6 of them about a year ago. 1 lost power. The USB cable melted itself inside the USB port at the camera. 1 of them would record, but wouldn't let me view live video. And Surprise, when I tried to unplug it the USB cable, it had welded itself to the USB port as well.The online video storage only gives you 12 seconds of video,very 5 minutes. If you want to save useful video, you will have to add an SD card. Coupled with its poor "motion detection", you're going to be very disappointed. Let me tell you about motion detection. It's not true motion detection. It detects changes in the video pixels. It will record every insect, animal, moving plant and stray beam of light. But it has no interest in human beings, especially the ones committing crimes. Let me splain... One of them is pointed at the parking lot of my business. This thing records the cast of headlight beams on objects of every car that passes by, but doesn't catch cars driving right in front of its face. Someone borrowed my shop truck. I wanted to find out who. There was no video of the truck ever leaving! Nor did it capture the fuel being siphoned from my truck, the graffiti being painted on the trailer, theft of my jerry cans, creeper being stolen, the time someone dumped a water heater at our back door... In most of these instances, zero seconds of video. I once saw a person's leg enter the scene, then it gets cut off. At my house, I have one at the gate aimed at the street. It loves insects flying by at night, spider webs in the wind, my neighbor's headlights 200 feet away... But last night, between the insects and spiderwebs, I got video of a dog peeing on my ficus at 3:45 AM. 20 minutes later when a dude went through my mailbox 30 feet away, no video. My neighbor's "cheap Chinese camera" detected motion and recorded it at 4:05 AM. I have landscape lights beaming up the palm plant under the mailbox. So, it can't tell me it was too dark.These things are a total disappointment.Update: My office trailer was broken into. There is one camera outside the trailer, and down at the door in which they broke in. There is another camera inside the trailer. NEITHER camera caught the breakin. As soon as the alarm went off, the culprits must have grabbed the nearest object, which was a welder, and took off. Neither camera reported any event. The very next evening, one of my trucks was broken into. There is a wyze cam 10 feet away pointed down at the trucks. Once again, the camera didn't even report any event. But all night, it was catching headlights of traffic going through the alley and dust flying in front of the camera. Utterly useless!

title: Good product, HORRENDOUS customer service
body: The camera works well. Easy enough to set up, clear picture, good night vision, etc. All the features described work just as advertised. However.....WYZE customer service is the worst I have ever experienced.The free use of the camera gives you only 12 seconds of recording per event, after which there is a 5 minute wait until the next motion capture. Utterly useless. So, you can subscribe for a mere $1.49 a month per camera to their CMC (Complete Motion Capture) service, starting with a 14 day free trial, which you get without having to enter any credit card information. 5 minutes at a time per event, with no wait in between. Splendid. I signed up. When the 14 days were up, I got an email notice and a link to the WYZE website to subscribe to the service. Great. Only...I could only get to the free 14 day trial sign up screen, which created a popup listing my camera. The camera was already selected but there was nothing to click to submit it, proceed to a subscription screen, nothing. All I could do was close the popup, which left me on the trial screen. And here's where the crappy customer service comes in.First of all, there is no toll free number to call. Annoying, especially when my smartphone is a Tracfone and wasting expensive minutes sitting on hold is not an option. So it was off to their chat feature if I wanted real time support. Good thing I wasn't on my Tracfone, because this guy had to keep running off to a "product specialist" to figure out why their system wouldn't let me subscribe. I asked him to have someone call me, but of course, this is not possible. A "specialist" would email me, though. A few days later, I got an email offering a month's free trial and providing a link to sign up. Groovy, I think. They've corrected the error. I follow the link. Guess where I land. That's right. The ol' 14 day free trial signup.I go to their billing page and enter my information and credit card, just in case this is the glitch. But no joy.So, I contact Amazon, explain the situation, and Amazon's amazing customer service comes through for me. The rep enters a refund for the full amount of the camera and the micro disk I bought to go with it, even though the disk is stuck in the camera and I had thrown the packaging away.The moral of my story is, if you get this camera and you can manage to subscribe to the CMC service, great. It will serve you well. If not, make sure you have plenty of time and patience to spend trying to get help from WYZE. Good luck, my friends.

title: You get what you pay for.  Super bad lag on Moto G6 Play.
body: I guess you get what you pay for. I bought three of these cameras from Amazon to set up around the house. Earlier this year, we got a Reolink C2 Pro for my work place to use in a class room. We went with that vs Wyze since I read and was told the audio quality was really poor, and we needed a cam with superb audio quality. I watched a bunch of YouTube videos on smart cam reviews, and one guy responded by saying to get the Reolink vs Wyze. So we did.For the house, I figured I didn't need great audio quality. So, I bought a bunch of these cheap Wyze V2 cams. Holy hell, when I first set up one with my Moto G6 Play, the lag and frame rate was so insanely bad, I thought I was being punked. I tested on various wifi routers, I updated the camera firmware, I tried a bunch of settings and video quality. It was horrendous. Like, 10 to 15 seconds of lag with frame rate in the 5 fps range. I read a bunch of reddit posts, and I downgraded to an older version of the app. It became a lot better, but the lag was still several seconds at times, although the frame rate was much better. With hardware acceleration enabled, the response time was MUCH better. Nearly real time. However, a new issue cropped up. I was getting a LOT of dropped frames. It would stream in real time, then just completely stop streaming, then stream again. Anyways, as a last ditch, I got a new iPad from work to try.On an iPad, the streaming is much more better. There is about 2 seconds of lag. Bit rate is still the same, though, 100+ kbps. So, it's likely the Moto G6 Play. However, testing the Reolink C2 Pro, I get just 1 second of lag even at 4 MP quality -- near 2k resolution. The quality is just much better and the frame rate is pretty awesome. The bit rate is going full 800 kbps. On the Moto G6 Play, I get the same response time and frame rate.Over all, the Reolink just has much better features. The playback is much more intuitive. I could never understand how the playback feature worked on the Wyze app with the time date stamps. Even recording continuously, I couldn't get the right playbacks. It was horrible. I also like Reolink's software much more. You can black out certain areas of the feed that may be considered sensitive. It has an option to upload via FTP to an external server for storage. It's just all around a more robust camera. I highly recommend it over this.

title: Pretty impressive, minor annoyance with app: EDITED, Rating Lowered
body: I'm generally very pleased with this little camera. I have it in my garage keeping watch over one of my toys. I check it regularly (most just to look at the nice toy). For the money, it's pretty remarkable, actually. Considering getting a few more, for hunting cabin, etc.My one complaint is - every time you access the camera with the app, it has to "re-authenticate", or something like that. So there is a delay of several seconds while it does that. You can't just open the app and immediately see what the camera sees. That little delay *could* be critical in some circumstances. I'm hoping this is something the company can improve.EDIT 9/30/20: Well, I guess I was too optimistic, as this thing turns out to have problems. Mainly, the notifications are not working right, and haven't been for some time now. I get a ping sound notifying me of movement, and the app shows the little red dot next to "Events", meaning there should be a video of the movement. But when you go there, it says "Nothing here to see." This seems to happen about half the time when I get a notification sound. Also, I get pop-ups on the phone saying I need to update the firmware, but then when I try, it says "You are current" (or something like that). In short, the app is all screwed up and has been for weeks.On top of that, this camera's sibling, the wireless outdoor camera, barely works at all and doesn't work at least 80% of the time. It just won't make a connection - even when you put the camera three feet from the base with no wall in between. And on the rare occasions when it actually connects (I know it's not the router etc, and installation is proper, as it does work sometimes), about half the time it only connects for a few seconds, and then it either stops or causes the app to crash and shut down.And because they marketed that one "privately" (to customers who had purchased this camera), there is nowhere to "review" it. When you email them, all you get are canned replies, suggesting things like "check your router" etc. And they won't accept a return after 30 days "in original condition" (it took more than that for me to find the time to install the thing), and even after I email them repeatedly with detailed info... same canned responses.I would avoid this company. The products are unreliable and the CS is non-existent.

title: You DON'T always get what you pay for.
body: I got this to view my enclosed front porch, and get phone alerts because Amazon and UPS drivers fail to ring the doorbell when they leave packages sitting on my porch, and I get sketchy looking people claiming to be selling stuff all the time (possibly casing my house) so it was also for security. The maximum distance from the camera of anything, was only about 8 feet.I'm not sure why this cam gets so many good reviews, but my experience was not very good. The image quality is about 420p NOT 1080p HD. It's about as bad as 1990's webcams, LOL. The porch is lit at night, so night vision wasn't needed, so it was turned off. ANYTHING trips the motion detector. I had it mounted high up, on a covered porch with walls on three sides, and literally no way for it to see cars going by on the street, or people on the sidewalk. Tree leaf shadows moving, faint car headlights hitting low on a brick wall adjacent to the porch from cars turning a corner 75 feet away, a leaf blowing around, rabbits, cats, a fly buzzing around. But then again notifications were intermittent, so constant false alarms. and then nothing when the Amazon driver or salesman walks up on the porch LOL. I set the motion detection box to just on the porch, and it worked about 50% of the time. Alerts pushed to phone over wifi are sometimes immediate, sometimes very slow, sometimes never happen. Unit had a constant problem with disconnecting itself from the router and having to be reset saying that the wifi signal wasn't available. Resetting required pushing a button on the bottom of the camera, which was not at all convenient. I have smart home porch light switches which use the same 2.4G wifi signal, one literally 3 feet from the camera and have had no problem whatsoever with it. There was nothing wrong with the wifi. The phone app is stupid and hard to use. When you want to look at the video of what tripped the motion sensor, you can see it in the thumbnail, but can't find it on the playback to zoom in and see it. It seems to mark the timeline with a red line, but when you play it back, it goes right past it and there's nothing there. (and yes I bought a 32Gb memory card for it) So I tossed it in the trash, and am now looking into alternate cameras. So about the only thing it has going for it IMO, is that it's cheap. Maybe you'll have better luck with it.

title: Tried Twice, Couldn't Get a Working Camera
body: I'd really like to give this camera more than one star, I really like the company, and they have a pretty good app, but both cameras I tried had issues.The first camera I ordered worked fine, but would not recognize that a SD card was inserted. I was using a new SD card, recommended by Amazon, and I did check to see it met the specs (32GB, Class 10, etc.). I contacted Wyze customer support, and made it clear that I had tried reformatting the card, and that the card works in every other device I put it into. Their answer was that I needed to use a Wyze branded memory card. Complete BS, but OK. Also, Wyze will not support or do any type of exchange for hardware issues, unless purchased direct from them, so I returned to Amazon, took a chance, and re-ordered another camera.Replacement camera arrives a few days later, pop in the memory card that the first camera would not recognize, and wouldn't you know it, it works just fine. So it does not need to be a Wyze branded card after all. Everything seems to be working so I move the camera out to the porch where I plan to install it. Plug it in, and it won't connect to my network. I reset the camera, reset the modem... nothing. I look at my phone, and it's showing full strength wifi signal. So i come back into my living room, power up the camera, it connects, so I look at the status on the app, 56% wifi signal. I'm physically within 12ft of the wifi router, moving further than about 20ft and I lose the signal and drop the connection. The first Wyze camera I had would read 80% signal messing around with it outside my house, I can only assume something is wrong with the new camera, bad wifi antenna or receiver, or whatever, but I give up.Like I said at the beginning, I really wanted to like this camera; for the price, it has good picture quality, a pretty full-featured app, free cloud storage, and seems to have an active developer team. None of those thing matter however, if the hardware does not work out of the box. I gave Wyze a second chance, and that's enough for me.I ordered another camera, for about the same cost. The app isn't as good, motion tracking isn't as good, picture quality is close, but not as good as Wyze, but you know what... I plugged it in, put in my SD card, and it just worked.

title: DO NOT BUY IF USING IPHONE
body: There is no way (at present) for owners of an iPhone to get notifications WITH A DIFFERENT SOUND other than what they already get 20 times a day from news alerts, etc. Who wants to be checking their iPhone every time they get a notification? Not me!I, along with thousands of Waze cam owners, want a DIFFERENT NOTIFICATION SOUND when our cameras detect a person or movement. I checked the Waze Forum and found this has been a long-time ongoing problem with hundreds of requests to fix it, yet, for some reason there is no fix- or even a promise of fixing it.I also learned that Android phone owners DO NOT have this problem. They can set their phone's notification sound to anything they desire! But not so with iPhone users. I assume there's a problem with Apple and the app store here, but really have no clue. In a longish chat with Wyze this morning I asked TWICE when this feature would be added to the cameras and the customer service guy there refused to even attempt an answer. That means it's likely not going to happen anytime soon. Thus my ONE star rating.As one user on their forum stated, "This camera is only good for a baby monitor!" because of this omission.Yes, set-up was fairly quick. The Wyze app is pretty good, except hard to read with it's light words. I figured how to add the cameras to my wife's phone (the app won't work on a desktop, apparently.)You likely won't be able to see identifying features on a person's face in a video IF THAT PERSON IS MOVING. It's too blurry. Still action is clear. Night vision is limited to around 10-12 feet from the camera. I am now getting "Error 07" on about 1 of every 5 video captures, where it won't play the recorded video. Hope that is fixed soon.Paying around $15 per year PER CAMERA allows that camera to detect "persons" as opposed to just motion, so I put it on my driveway cam, plus I added a 32gb micro SD card so it will record 24/7 instead of in 25-30 second increments with a 5 minute "cooldown" in between. I recommend spending the extra $3 to get a HD (heavy duty) micro SD card meant for long use in extreme temperatures like outside cameras need. I didn't even have to format mine.In conclusion, the price is right for what you get in this Wyze camera, so long as you have an ANDROID phone!

title: Pieces of Junk.  Terrible support and Dishonest company.
body: So I had to outright change my review. After just a month of use, I have definitely seen the light on how much these cameras are not ready for consumers.Let's start with the camera and the app. The cameras are cute, small and easily installed. The mount is crappy. It's a magnet, so if you bump it, accidentally nudge the cable or your pets touch it, your view will change. If there is a vibration on a wall from an AC unit, the camera will fall. The app looks sleek, but it lacks features.The connectivity is absent. Terrible! 9 out of 10 attempts to connect are met with either errors or a reattempt. Even when the cameras connect, the video lags and more often disconnects. You get alerts, but the clips are ~12 seconds long and you only get one of those every 3-5 minutes. I have had multiple instances where the cameras didn't even catch a maintenance man walking through a door. You can spend an extra $10 and get a class 10 MicroSD card, but that's no picnic. The camera has to be connected to utilize playback through the app (see above comments about connectivity). So to view clips, you have to uninstall the card (see comments about mount and shifting view). Then if you get that far, you have to compile 60 second clips into the length of video you are seeking.The image quality is poor. Since connectivity is so terrible, it appears that the streaming video is often defaulting to SD, even with all the setting to HD. The night vision image quality is even worse. Audio is lackluster too. The mic has its gain set so high that you can hear static constantly during playback. Unless the sounds are loud enough they are missed. The speaker isn't worth even trying to review.All of this and the company released a second version, V2, just 5 months after launch. They completely pulled V1 off the market and had a pre-order for V2, which is just now going out to early pre-orders (delayed at least twice). This company is not ready for market. They are inexperienced and hacking away at a market that they are ill equipped to compete it. Spend the extra dollars and buy a reputable product. This product is mass produced and then they have placed their firmware and app on top. It's a joke. Move on, buy something else.

title: Doesn't connect, then connects, then disconnects...all on install.
body: Despite the qualified reviews, I decided to try this camera and be one of the people who had luck with it and (hopefully) I'd leave a great review.Unfortunately, I've spent 45 minutes in the setup process, and it's failed at almost every step. Sometimes inconsistently and bizarrely.I unpackaged the camera (great packaging, by the way), installed the app on my iPhone, and plugged the cam in to the wall. I inserted the microSDHC card, and all was going without a hitch. I opened the app, and possibly the first sour note was that it instructed me to remove the plastic protective film from the front of the camera. It even put up a nice graphic on the app. But there was no protective film on the front of my camera. Possibly a re-furbished camera?Then it wouldn't connect to my WiFi network. At first it said the password was incorrect (it wasn't), but after five minutes it announced that "Setup was complete." I hadn't done anything, but I was hopeful it simply fixed itself. I tried to livestream from the Wyze app, but after another five minutes it told me it needed to upgrade the firmware. So I tapped on "Upgrade" and it announced it couldn't connect to my device.So I'm stuck. I tried calling Wyze, but the pleasant recording on the phone tells me nobody is available to answer, that I should send email for support, and then hangs up without a voicemail option. Went to the Wyze site and submitted a support ticket, to which I got an automatic response promising a technician would address my problem as soon as they could. Now I wait.I'm glad I bought it from Amazon. I'll send it back tomorrow for a full refund it if Wyze doesn't contact me today. For $26 or so, I'm not worried either way, but I'm disappointed it doesn't work so far. Would have been nice.I'll post an update soon.UPDATE: I submitted a help ticket in email, and filled out their form on their website. That was almost a week ago. Two days ago I got a message from them apologizing for the delay in responding. I haven’t heard from them since. I mailed the camera back and got a refund already to my credit card from Amazon. I love Amazon, and will avoid this camera.

title: an old cell phone does the same thing...
body: sorry, but this cam isn't quite ready for prime time. if you just want to watch the dog, buy one. if you want reliable, real-time notifications of people on your property, buy something else.we've had 4 cams up for nearly a month and here's what i've found:the most annoying thing is the 5-minute cooldown. if a bird or bug flies past a cam, the cam detects motion, then stops detecting for 5 minutes. so it DOESN'T send a notification of the guy who just stole a package from my front porch right after a bird flew by.2nd most annoying: notifications of motion arriving seconds, minutes, or even hours after the event. the notifications might arrive 20 seconds after an "event" or they might arrive an hour later.the 3rd most annoying thing: limitations of the field for motion detecting. i get a rectangle. that's it. if i want to eliminate the cam detecting motion every time a car drives down a street (and it will), i can only drag the corners of the detection zone to make a rectangle. one corner can't be moved independent of the others. hundreds of these cam owners have asked wyze about this with no response.4th most annoying: trying to find and review an event of importance. heaven forbid you record constantly instead of recording just events. you can't view a specific time, you must scroll through the recording on a very small phone screen to find the ups guy dropping off a package at your door.5th: no PC support. good luck trying to view your recording on a pc. without finding and loading a resource-hogging emulator, you only get support for mobile devices.6th: takes FOREVER to load live recording. doesn't matter if it's a mobile device or echo show, it can take up to 15 seconds or longer to display the live cam view. by that time, the guy trying your car doors to see if they're unlocked is long gone. and if a feather floated by the cam, you won't get a notice that he was there at all (see the #1 most annoying thing).wyze would rather make more money selling light bulbs (like THAT'S a new thing) than make what they already sell work better.yes, wyze's cam is cute. yes, it's cheap.but you get what you pay for.

title: Camera does not work without an internet connection.
body: RECENT UPDATE:I had been running two of these cameras with minor issues since Wyze replaced the original camera (twice to get a single functional device) and as such I decided to order another to add to my setup.Suffice to say this has been a miserable experience. The new camera causes the old camera to drop the live stream repeatedly, rendering both cameras useless until the new one is disconnected.These cameras are obviously decent quality for the price, but this vendor cannot seem to get a grip on the firmware issues plaguing an otherwise awesome product that SHOULD be fully functional with standard RTSP protocols and NOT tethered to cloud based services to work properly.ALL versions of their posted firmware did nothing to resolve the issue, I filed (yet another) support ticket on the issue, I will not be updating this further, my advice is spend the extra money and get a better product with reliable firmware.UPDATE: Wyze has contacted me and is sending a replacement camera as this one is clearly not functioning as advertised. Hopefully the replacement camera will function as it should. I will update my review when I have the new camera and can verify it's functionality.Bought this camera for use in a location that does not have a 24/7 internet connection, with the intent to buy more if it worked for my purpose.According to their literature, and their website, this camera needs to be setup with an internet connection, and can then be used offline to record images and video to the internal SD Card.This is NOT the case. The camera will ONLY record video when it is online, and connected to the Internet.I spent more than an hour with tech support 'troubleshooting' which includes installing beta firmware and a beta android app and nothing changes the fact that this camera is entirely useless without an internet connection.So if you have an internet connection, and it will be ONLINE 24/7, you're good to go, otherwise I suggest you look elsewhere.It's a shame, the camera has a good quality picture and the nightvision works very well.Very disappointed in my purchase.

title: your camera is useless once the company is sold
body: The app asks for so many permissions. The app should only need to have a permission at set up to set up the camera. The camera once connects to the router, the app no longer needs anything, other than sync with the camera using the barcode (as a security measure) to connect to the camera to retrieve video information.The camera should not send any information nor video anywhere whatsoever.
