If the users choose to do so, let them have the option for that. That's all. Why the app needs microphone access? With all these security breach, it's almost a sure thing this will be hacked. And also with all those dependency, your camera is useless once the company is sold, or decide they don't support it anymore. You paid for it, you owned it, but sorry, someone else decide how long you can use it.I thought you stick the memory card in there, and turn on and it works. That's all. If you want to access using phone app, then allow internet connection on it. What about no phone app? Can just a computer connect to it and record the video or display the video?This camera is very intrusive.Here's the app's permissions, do you believe it?WyzeWyze Labs, Inc.This app has access to:Photos/Media/Filesaccess USB storage filesystemread the contents of your USB storagemodify or delete the contents of your USB storageStorageread the contents of your USB storagemodify or delete the contents of your USB storageMicrophonerecord audioWi-Fi connection informationview Wi-Fi connectionsDevice & app historyretrieve running appsPhoneread phone status and identityDevice ID & call informationread phone status and identityOtherreceive data from Internetview network connectionsallow Wi-Fi Multicast receptionconnect and disconnect from Wi-Fifull network accesschange your audio settingsprevent device from sleepingview network connectionschange system display settingsconnect and disconnect from Wi-Fifull network accesscontrol vibrationprevent device from sleeping

title: Seriously, don't bother.  Shoddy faulty hardware and apps.  Customer service is unhelpful.
body: Previous headline: "Got the 2-pack. One has no microphone audio. Both have connection problems."Original review:I ordered the 2-pack of Wyze Cam v2 for about $50. Powered up the first one and connected to it via the Wyze app on my iPhone. (Connecting means establishing a WiFi link from the phone to the camera so I can see its video, hear its audio, change its settings, etc.) Sometimes it connects, sometimes it doesn't. Sometimes when I change the resolution or other setting the camera disconnects. A few times I was completely unable to connect/reconnect until I restarted the iPhone app and/or cycled power to the camera. Basically I found the connection process to be buggy and unreliable. In addition, after testing for a while I realized I had no sound incoming from the first camera. Seems like a hardware failure with the microphone. So essentially the first camera is physically broken on arrival and needs to be replaced.I powered up the second camera of the 2-pack and had similar problems with connectivity but at least the microphone is working and I can hear incoming sound. The connectivity problems persist with both cameras even with the camera firmware and iOS app updated to the latest versions and a 32GB microSD class 10 card installed in each one.Sadly, it seems you get what you pay for. I've opened a support ticket with Wyze to see what they can do about replacing the camera with the bad microphone. Based on how that goes I'll either keep them or return them to Amazon.UPDATE: I contacted support. I told them what was wrong with lots of details. They basically ignored the details and asked for everything all over again. Told them again. Sent more info during some back-and-forth. Finally they said they'd put me in touch with their team for a replacement. And then... nothing. Haven't heard from them since. Haven't gotten a replacement. They completely dropped the ball. So basically their customer service is on par with their product: Cheap, shoddy and broken. I'm going to return the product to Amazon and leave up this 1-star review so people will know what they're getting into.

title: Horrible customer service
body: TLDR - If you get a bad camera just return it immediately. DO NOT attempt to work through their technical support as they will just give you the run around.The camera seemed to have good picture quality when it worked. However, it kept losing connection and giving me errors. I contact customer support and exchanged a few emails on what to do to troubleshoot. I'm an engineer and never typically need to contact technical support. Most of what they had said to do, I already did (hard reset, delete the app and reinstall, reboot my router, change router configurations, remove SD, etc., etc.). I even went as far as to buy a new router even though it is the only device that has issues on my network. It was clear to me there was an issue that could not be resolved and likely a hardware problem.I sent them logs, continued to respond to requests, even re-answered some of the same questions over and over again. I kept getting auto-generated emails ("We recently wrote to you about your request (#156753) but we haven't heard back from you. Please let us know if your issue has been resolved, or provide any additional information so that we can continue helping you.") --- despite the fact that I had replied, and was waiting on their responses.I have since heard back that their engineers reviewed the logs and think I haven't waited long enough to let it connect. I told them this is patently false. I've connected a number of times, the connection is unstable and drops. If I didn't let it connect long enough, it was because it sent one of about 3 error codes that don't provide any useful information. (onnection camera by secure channel (1/3) Try to connect: 2 times. ; please try to power cycle the cameray (error code -60) ... i've also seen error code-42 and error-code-90 ).I actually came into Amazon orders today to return it, only to find out yesterday was the last day. I'm hoping Wyze will send me a new camera (as I've asked them to 3 times) otherwise I'm just out $25. Not the end of the world. I'm more ticked off I've wasted hours of my life trying to fix their broken hardware, and explain to them that it's just not working.

title: Good features that are unreliable
body: Camera has horrible picture and audio recording quality. High resolution doesn't mean much if the bit rate is low, which can be fixed in software. Customers should be given control of what bit rate to record at. Any semi-loud sounds gets distorted. May be mic has too much gain? This is a cheap camera so I can forgive these things but the features that you need to be reliable is what is making this camera really bad as a whole. Those features are motion detection and alerts, and playback.Motion detection is still so buggy even with the Nov 15, 2019 firmware update and does not catch the most obvious, important things but never fails to capture useless motion. I have this pointing at my driveway and it never alerts me of my car when I drive into it, and it has failed many times to alert me of people that walk right in front of it, like delivery drivers. When I view the playback, which is hit or miss sometimes, it clearly shows the green detection squares around my car and the people it fails to send alerts for.Playback gets laggy when trying to move the time slider back or forth to get to the wanted time of recording. Some times app crashes and you have to do this multiple times. If you make multiple moves on the time slider to get to the correct time, the feed becomes unresponsive and takes time to move through all your individual time-moves before finally getting to the final move. And good luck getting audio to work while you do all this. Sometimes it needs to be toggled a few times for it to work. In addition to this, some playback of important detections are choppy and missing a lot of video frames, and even sped up, so you end up with a useless recording.This is all observed on the local network by the way with just a few devices on the network and during low to no bandwidth usage.Also I think the camera should record at 20 fps (compared to the 15 fps) at least for the entire (not just 12 secs) duration of detections.If these issues don't get fixed soon I'll be returning them. What's the point of owning security alert cameras if they can't alert of and/or record events consistently?

title: No access to easy remote viewing(as of 10/01/20)
body: So this camera has amazing night vision which I love and is the only reason I'm not returning it(as long as the features I'm using don't get removed) and a pretty good picture and motion detection. If they give free access to downloading recordings and modify the way to review recordings I'll end up updating it probably to 5 star.Edit - So they finally told me what the features of the subscription are. The recording for 12 seconds is only once every 5 mins other wise you have to pay 1.25 a month(not much but there are other cameras that offer that for free). and person detection as well as cloud access. While I could see doing the 1.25 for unlimited could storage making me pay for what I would consider reasonable features on a night vision smart camera. I am returning them.To start with I'm not sure what features are normal and which ones are subscription yet, if needed I will update this with more information, during setup it says oh welcome to your free 2 week trial of premium services... but it doesn't tell you which of the services are premium...The set up is pretty easy and straight forward, I do wish there was an app for the computer but not a deal breaker the android app can be used off of the home wifi network which is good(but can be a bit spotty not sure if it's due to my network or the connection).It does allow local storage but the reviewing is not great. The navigation is terrible, you can select a day but then you have to scroll across a timeline and there is only play or pause. I've had others in this price range that at least let you have the ability to get the events in a list and choose that way. Also currently there is no way to download the recordings to your phone, you have to pop out the SD card and then you can transfer them to your computer.If you are looking for alerts of motion detection and live viewing this is great, if you are trying to find out which of your 3 puppies are peeing on the carpet at night it's not great but it's ok for the price(my old camera pooped out on me and is not available currently)

title: Love the price - Hate the camera - and the Company
body: UPDATE: It stopped connecting to WiFi. I emailed Wyze describing the problems and they basically said, "What do you want us to do about it? Fortunately it was still in the return window, so I did. AVOID AT ALL COSTS!--------I do see a lot of people like it, so maybe this is just a bad unit. I'll try exchanging it for another before giving up on it entirely.The Proso The Price is good.o The Setup is easy.o The video quality is very good.o The IR lights give a good "total darkness" Picture.The Conso There are no instructions so you have to do a lot of trial and error to get it working the way you want. It took me a while to figure out what "Motion Tagging" is and how to turn it off.o It only comes in white, making it hard to be discreet It is unusable as a "Nanny" camera.o The WiFi connection is flaky.o It loses the WiFi Connection after just a few minutes of being connected. Get used to "Connection has timed out (error code (20011)o The video freezes up whether you are viewing it on a smartphone or on an Amazon Show The camera is only about 7 feet from the WiFi Router. Get used to "Buffering" on an Amazon Showo It seems to disconnect other smart devices from WiFi if they happen to be near it.o The lag time is very bad making the two way audio worthless. (even when it actually stays connected)o It can store video on an SD card, but it creates one file for every minute.o There doesn't seem to be a way to keep its video from going to its cloud I read that security is a major concern in that they are based in China. I would prefer being able to save to DropBox or other cloud storage that can be trusted more AND, be able to access the data on the SD Card without having to physically remove the card and insert it into a computero There doesn't seem to be a way to control or delete video from the cloud There are things I would want to keep private

title: Will not connect to any of my 2.4 g WiFi networks. Does not find SSID though 6 ft from router!
body: I was hopeful for these, given the positive reviews. I basically ignored the ones for set up, as most of those I figured were folks that couldn’t. I guess I’m one of them.My network, with a modem feeding a tri band WIFI router that feeds 3 dual band routers configured as access points, has tons of bandwidth and signal strength. But still I set new devices up about 6 feet from router, then when verified, move them to the AP with strongest signal. With smart home devices, IoTs, etc, I have at any time 80-90 devices online.Theses would not connect. Would not even recognize the SSID of my router. (Yes, the 2.4 one. I got do tired of them telling me to check it). They do not do a clean set up. They do not find your SSIDs through their antenna before going through set up, rather their app suggests the one you are using for your set up device (I tried iPhone, iPad, and android phone). Then the app generates a barcode the camera scans, and that, presumably, sends the setup coding to the camera. What could possibly go wrong there? Well, somewhere between my using the SSIDs and password that at time of setup was collectively working on 49 WIFI devices, and scanning their own barcode, something got lost.There is no way to do a manual, enter-your-own stuff like almost all my other devices use, these are worthless.No, I’m not spending time reconfiguring my router as the troubleshooting website gets to. I’m not replacing my olde AC68 router AP with a new AC68 AP, nor am I going to spend another two hours screwing with a black box that won’t talk to my system.FYIWhile I was working on this, my grandson (9) came into room, got bored watching me, took out his iPad mini 2, asked me for the password for the router in the room, input it and started playing his NETFLIX.That’s about when I said, time to return these things I got this afternoon...Too much smoke and mirrors behind the curtain.Thanks for reading

title: Deleted recorded events from same day and beyond!
body: I really wanted to like this product. I saw a lot of great reviews and thought this would be an awesome product. When you look at the company’s value proposition, it’s perfect. Provide home security and automation products at an affordable price point.Install and setup went well. Fairly straight forward. Took about 15 minutes to get both cameras setup and install the app. The app is fairly intuitive, but could be easier to select how/when you want events recorded. Put that on the main menu instead of in the settings.The build quality is okay. I’ve had Ring and Arlo in the past and you can tell those are higher quality and heavier. These are light, but they get the job done. Your cameras shouldn’t need to be that tough - they just sit there all day.The fatal flaw with Wyze cams...they don’t record and keep events! It’s like a Seinfeld episode about taking a reservation but not keeping it! While I was at work today, I get a notification on my Apple Watch that says I have motion on my porch. So I open up my phone, go to events, takes longer than usual to load, and....nothing. Completely blank. I tried to reload the app - still no events. Tried to stream live and then go back to the events page - still no events. I pulled down in the events page to reload, and again nothing. And yes, in settings, I have set to record my camera on motion and sound.When you buy cameras for security or monitoring purposes, you expect them to work when you need them. All of the events over the past few days recorded and stored just fine. Until today. They were all wiped from my phone/account. I can only imagine if I were out of town and got a motion alert and went to check on the recorded event and found that there were no recordings from that day, or the previous 14 days.Not really sure how customer service could convince me to not send these back to Amazon...Sorry Wyze, really wanted to like these.

title: Worst Customer Policies I have ever seen from a company selling on Amazon
body: Update: They keep sending you an email to review their product, but once you click on one star review it can’t be submitted on their website, so instead I’m posting this on Amazon. Boycott this product!The product is ok for it's pricepoint. I was attracted to this product through YouTube and wanted to give it a try. I also bought a motion sensor through Wyze.com to see if maybe the additional sensor would improve the camera. I had alot of false positives and false negatives with the product. Little did I know that in order for the sensor to work, I also had to purchase a full starter kit (a bridge, two contact sensors and a motion sensor) so that the motion sensor could be added to the camera (the camera has a built in motion sensor but is poor). When I realized that I needed to buy a $20 starter kit, I decided I wanted to return the $10 motion sensor I just bought or at least see if there was an option to purchase the bridge that I needed. Well, they said that I needed to buy the full starter kit and they don't sell the bridge separately. So basically my budget camera, is now turning into more and more money. I can't return the sensor since I disposed the box last night (my own fault), and now I have a $10 piece of worthless sensor that cannot connect to anything in my house. I have had far better customer service with other companies who were understanding and charged me for items that I needed and never asked me to make extra wasteful purchases. I have tried my best to minimize my own waste footprint and it's frustrating to have to "recycle" a brand new product because it can't be used. I did notify wyze customer service I have no need for contact sensors as my whole entire house already has built in alarm sensors, I only bought the motion sensor to add to the wyze cam to see if it can improve motion detection. Customer service was good but customer policies in place are horrible and they need to be reviewed.

title: You Get What You Pay For!
body: I decided to give this company a shot because so many loved their cameras and price point. Sorry, but these cameras are not that great and there are SERIOUS issues with them:1. ) I have a camera that I cannot connect to reliably. I'd say 9 times out of 10 I get the error "Connection failed (error code 0)" The vendor tried to push this off on a network error or configuration. I'm a network engineer by trade and rest assured I'm not blocking protocols or ports related to this product.If you search the Wyze forums, you'll find many frustrated people with this error!I purchased another vendors camera and put it side by side with the Wyze and guess what? I can access it anytime I wish! Fix your issues Wyze!!!2. The night vision on this camera is terrible compared to other cameras with a slightly higher price point. I'll spend the extra money and get a GOOD picture.3. Wyze sold this camera on the premise that it had built in "motion detection". Once I got all these cameras setup, they made the announcement that this feature was being pulled because they apparently didn't impress the company who owns this patent enough to continue to provide this product.4. Next up on the motion detection is that their fix is to provide their own cloud infrastructure to serve this up. Coincidentally, this company just announced that they leaked a lot of consumer data because of terrible system administration practices on their end. While this issue was bouncing around in the forums for DAYS, it took 5 days before the CEO owned the issue and sent a notification to all consumers. What do we get for having our data made public? "Sorry, we'll do better". That's it!Sorry, but this company seems not ready for the big leagues and there remains some concern about their ties to China and where your data is going. I'm moving on, not selling my soul for a cheap camera...

title: SUCKER DEAL - ACCOUNT is free for 14 days, not ea video storage - then there's a monthly fee!
body: They use very sneaky wording that can be taken 2 ways. It seems like each video can be stored for 14 days without a charge. WRONG! You can use the thing at all for 14 days without charge - then there's a monthly fee. I wondered why they wanted me to open an account when I didn't care about cloud storage, just live view.But there is NO way to set it up just for live view like there is with the Blink mini cam - which is the same price, works with Alexa and Ring, and takes about 5 minutes to set up with NO account and live view wherever you get a wifi signal with your phone or tablet.To do the same with with the WYZE takes quite some time to set up and connect - and then you suddenly need a account - an after 2 weeks of use will be charged a monthly subscription fee.If you DON'T open an account thew app simply shows the WYZE logo - and nothing else with no menu item - forever. The only thing you can do is close the app - and NOT use the camera.Very, very deceptive - and as others have noted the security of your information if you DO open an account is just about zero, and on servers in China!this is one of the few times I discarded packaging materials, as the number of positive reviews unfairly raised my confidence. So I can't return it.I guess I'll rip the base off and use the magnet on my refrigerator. The camera will be trashed - or maybe put on display after I leave it on the bottom of one of my parrot's cages for appropriate "decorating" first.Buy the Blink instead. They don't have the storage unit available yet, but if, like me, all you want is periodic live view it's perfect, the same price, and no monthly fee is required to use it.Amazon should FORCE WYZE to change their wording, because it's not really buyers' fault they get suckered - you just have to read ALL the purchase info before completing installation!

title: Had High Hopes, Decent Quality, Serious Software Issues
body: I regret buying 5 of the Wyze cameras. I had high hopes after buying the first one and bought 4 more. I have to admit setup is a breeze and the picture quality is very good for the price. You get alerts on your phone in less than 10 seconds, but the alerts are a major pain in the butt. I have had my cameras over 2 months and delete the alerts daily. The problem is alert thumbnails and videos mysteriously reappear on your phone a few weeks later by the hundreds. One day you will have nothing in your picture/video gallery on your phone and the next there will be hundreds that you deleted weeks ago. I have to wonder how secure their system is when things that have been deleted come back to haunt you weeks later?I spoke to tech support a few weeks ago and followed their suggestions. They said they thought this issue had been fixed. That was at least 3 weeks ago. I got hundreds of deleted notifications back today. A friend who has a camera looked at his phone today and his deleted notifications had also returned.A new software version was released today. At first glance it is a step backward. I have not spent much time with the new version, but it appears you can't delete blocks of notifications and have to do it one at a time.Though the cameras are only $26 each with shipping, the price quickly adds up. I would have been better off paying a little more and getting something with the bugs worked out such as the Arlo cameras.Yes all my cameras have the most recent firmware and I have installed every software update in the past two months. And as stated above, I spoke to tech support a few weeks ago and they thought the issue had been resolved.If I had not recycled the packaging I would send them all back for a refund. I will probably chalk this up as a loss and recycle them at the next electronic recycling in my town.

title: Cheap & unreliable, but you get what you pay for
body: These cameras are nearly useless even as a basic remote “security” camera; it is not nearly reliable enough to be to the foundation of a home security monitoring system.Picture quality is good enough (but not great), as is the night vision. The primary issue I have is with the fickle responsiveness. I don’t know if it’s a problem with the app, hardware, or both, but more often than not, I will see at least one of the cameras as being offline and unreachable. It will magically reconnect whenever it feels like it. If I hear activity that I want to check on, more often than not the camera will be “offline”, or will just refuse to connect. Changing camera settings in the app is equally unreliable, where it takes uncomfortably long for the individual camera settings to load, if they load at all. It makes it difficult to determine if you’ve actually configured the camera and whether the settings have stuck or not.Reviewing video is equally hit-or-miss; some captured motion event videos will appear but are unviewable, with a vague message saying the the video doesn’t exist. Why is it visible in the events list, then?Also, the motion detection regularly fails to trigger whenever there is movement that should trigger it (probably due to a camera being sporadically offline). The cameras cannot be relied upon to capture everything that happens in front of it.I don’t know if I missed something in the settings, but there seems to be no way to adjust the recording time after motion or sound detection, it appears to be fixed at 12 seconds. UPDATE: I discovered that you have to pay extra for a subscription to get that perk.These cameras are adequate if you’re on a budget and you absolutely need something, but be aware that they will eventually fail you when you need them. You get what you pay for.

title: WORST PURCHASE EVER!!!!!
body: 2020.12.01 update: DON’T WASTE YOUR MONEY!!!!This camera lost its place and I can’t get it to reboot or reconnect to the Wi-Fi. It is a paperweight. It is rubbish. Just don’t buy this piece of crap, you will regret it forever.Also, the motion sensor feature, is only free for one year. Then, if you don’t subscribe, every time you try to open the camera you get hit with an ad to try to force you to buy their service. This is the worst POS I have ever bought from Amazon2020.02.24 update. Upgrading to 4 stars. Learning more. I installed a 32gb micro sd card. Still only get 12 second recordings on motion. But I also now have several days of playback video. So I'm much happier about this purchase now.I got 2 Wyze cameras because a friend said he had them and liked them. The smaller unit that doesn't move, is okay. The day vision is really good. The night vision isn't very good unless you have some good night lights. The motion sensor is alright, and the Wyze camera that swivels follows the action pretty good.What I really am 100% upset with is, it only records 12 seconds. And, they're only made for indoor use. I have one aimed out my front window because my neighborhood has had problems with package and mail theft. The camera captured a delivery driver getting out of his van, going to the back door to retrieve the package... and then it just stopped. No actual footage of what happened. If it had been a thief, I wouldn't have had any help at all.I googled and went to Wyze's support website, and all I could find was, "Yup, 12 seconds. That's all you get. No plans to change that."I'd rather have purchased a proper system with indoor/outdoor cameras and a 1tb-4tb hard drive. Unless I'm monitoring the Wyze's all the time, they're really pretty much useless to me.

title: Caveat Emptor ...
body: I am updating my earlier “thumbs up” review and modifying it. Wyze issued firmware & app updates two days ago and since that time, their product is useless and doesn’t work at all. I am now revising my review to “caveat emptor” (let the buyer beware) and suggesting a hold against any consideration of purchasing anything from this company.Original Review ...I absolutely could not possibly be any happier with the WyzeCam product.I’ve owned the huge golf-course home with the ridiculously expensive security system installation and the monthly monitoring charges. But now I’m retired and live in a small resort studio. I don’t need the multi-thousand dollar security system but I would like some peace of mind when I’m away from home knowing my place is protected.I’ve got that now with my amazing WyzeCam. It works like a charm.The video picture quality is TOP NOTCH.The motion detector works perfectly.The app is perfect.One $25 WyzeCam covers the majority of my studio. If someone’s at my sliding glass front door, my WyzeCam catches them. It also catches any activity on the windows. For a secutity company installation, I’d pay over $1000 with a monthly monitoring fee of $15 - $40 dollars.My WyzeCam cost $25 and I couldn’t be happier.One tip: Set your Wyze app to be active the hours you’re away or for 24/7. Then when you come and go, simply toggle the motion detector and sound detector buttons on and off when you come and go. They don’t mention this in their printed materials but you’ll thank me for this tip after you start using your WyzeCam.You’re a fool if you don’t buy this. For $25, it’s a steal. 110% endorsement. Couldn’t be happier. All my neighbors are getting them too. Get one today.

title: Don't waste your money
body: I bought these because I didn't wanna spend the amount Nest was charging for there cameras and believe me, just spend the extra cash and get the nest cameras.I've had nothing but problems from day 1 with these, they never connected properly, they wouldn't turn off, no matter how many times you turn them off in the app, it would never work. I ended up getting a smart plug and plugging it into that just to get it to shut off. And that's the issue with these, I had to do so many work around just to get them to work properly.They would take forever to connect just to even look at them. You'll get a movement notification and got to look at it and you'll spend the next 10 mins going "just load! Come on! It was just working a second ago!" You constantly have to "power cycle" the cameras, which involves plugging it and plugging it back in which, again is very frustrating, and again you have to do all these work arounds just to get them to work properly.The app uses your Phone Audio instead of Media Audio on your phone to listen to the sound. So if your connected to any Bluetooth headphones or your vehicles Bluetooth, your car will always think your on a phone call and when your on a call on Bluetooth. It will cut out all the time. I tested this with the app installed and uninstalled and it is definitely the app.The cameras feel cheap which doesn't bother me, I don't care how they feel, I just want them too work.I ended up giving these away and spending the money and got the Nest Indoor cameras and never looked back.If you getting Wyze for home security, don't, it's not safe because they are a hassle to get to work right. You want something easy and something that just works. And these arn't it.

title: Horrible buy
body: So I loved it when I first got it Now a couple of months later I totally regret it. The cameras I had outside of my house with these covers on didn’t hold up. They fell off and the cameras got damaged. I emailed them because it was suppose to hold up obviously and it didn’t. They have been giving me the worst run around instead of just replacing what was broken due to false advertisement. I have email after email stating what they wanted me to do to ELECTRONICALLY prove that the cameras were in fact damaged RATHER THAN allowing me to mail the broken items in so they can examine it themselves to be sure that I was being truthful. This is by far the worst company to get cameras from. When you speak into the camera it’s a delay in addition to sounding horrible on the receiving end. There’s even a lag. I hadn’t even complained about that. I was genuinely concerned about the fact that my outdoor cameras weren’t truly protected and are now in fact broken. And now I have broken cameras and no replacements for them and as I stated in my email to them I HAVE HAD THESE LESS THAN A YEAR. These covers aren’t waterproof and obviously not sturdy because the cameras ended up with water in them and now 1 camera won’t turn on at all and the other sounds horrible when I plug it in. Again, they have yet to allow me to mail it back to them or replace the broken cameras that were damaged. At this point I don’t even want replacements, I just wanted to warn you guys about how much they don’t care if you spent your money on cameras and supposed waterproof covers , if they become damaged it’s on you. Not them. This cover was suppose to protect the camera IT DID NOT !!!!!!!!! Save your money!! And I left this same review under the SUPPOSED WATERPROOF covers for these cameras too.

title: New Monthly Fee for Features that used to be FREE
body: UPDATE DECEMBER 2020: I will never recommend Wyze Cams again to ANYONE. Recently they removed many features that originally just came with the purchase of your camera. You now have to pay a monthly fee to remove a five minute cooldown in-between auto-recording motion, ARE YOU *^@#%*^@ KIDDING ME!?!?!? WYZE IS A GARBAGE, MONEY GRABBING, TWO-FACED, POS COMPANY. These cameras used to be amazing (little buggy, but tolerable), and I recommended them to everyone, never again. *Previous review* Good overall value/product, but man the bugs these thing have are insane and seem to have the worst timing. When they want to work, they're great! However be aware that these little guys love to disconnect from your wifi, disable random features (like motion detection), and/or just flat out stop working at no particular time other than when you seem to need them the most. I can't tell you how many times I've played back video only to discover the camera decided to stop recording when what I was looking for occurred. Also, the device doesn't directly save to it's sd (I'm sure there's a technical word for this); i.e. I was watching a live stream from one of my cameras when I observed something I wanted to save/record to my phone. So I used the playback feature and started to record, and a couple seconds in, the video skips two seconds ahead and I miss what I needed to record!!! What kind of crap is that!?! It was perfect live. So what are you guys doing, sending the video to your cloud first before allowing the onboard memory to serve its purpose? Or is the software so poorly written that the majority or the features are just broken i.e. continuous loop record. I've owned my camera's for well over a year now and have had issues with them all at one point or another.

title: cant believe people are still buying these
body: I bought 5 of these thinking I could solve any issue since i know about networks and computers I'm done with this crap. here's my list.#1 cameras always loose connection#2 they only record for 12 seconds and take 5 minutes to start again so a breakin thief has plenty of time to get in and out without being recorded.#3 Outdoor camera needs to be charged every week not every 2 months so I have to climb a ladder to charge all the time.#4 my car got broken into and i have a camera right on the house 10 feet away and it did not record the thief. it did record me when I walked out in the morning. So they miss alot of motion.#5 Constant updating and rebooting. Why would you need to keep updating? because factory shipped cameras that were not tested enough and they wanted to save money and get us to let them know what to fix this is what software people do and we end up footing the bill for developers, Dah!#6 video viewing software is horrible on app.#7 Sim card for outdoor camera does not record video.#8 Sensitivity of recording events does not work try to set the camera so it doesn't pick up trees moving or shadows that don't work so if you have a windy day you will get 50 events of a tree moving.#9 You have to pay now if you want person detection, that was free when I bought this crap!#10 I could go on but I will stop here I've wasted enough time on this junk this was a good concept but poorly marketed and poor quality. If these cameras just recorded the 12 seconds and reset in a few seconds that would be fine but they made them this way so you would purchase subscription every company now wants us to send them $50.00 a month for something its getting stupid and greedy! Buy at your own Risk!

title: My expectations were WAY too high
body: As the title states, I expected way too much from this camera. Though it's not altogether unreasonable. As the product description states, I expected continuous recording (with SD card), motion detection, free cloud storage for 14 days, and easy setup. Half of those were true in my case.If i could BOLD this, I would -- SD CARDS ARE A REAL PROBLEM WITH THIS CAMERA. I purchased one of the ones recommended by numerous Wyze forum and Reddit posts. Sandisk cat10 32GB. Pretty standard card. Used the instructions on their website along with what people in the forums were saying. Couldn't get the cam to read the videos and pictures off the SD. I would pop it out and pop it into my laptop and find only a few images and clips there. So the cam wouldn't read what it's written to the card NOR would it properly continuously record to it. I then tried a high-end Samsung EVO card. Same thing. The next day this cam went back and I got a ring indoor cam. Seems like a LOT of people have trouble with SD cards in this thing. Wyze claims it's fixed but NOPE.Pro:Easy peasy to set up and mountSimple app interfaceCheapMotion detection was decentCons:CHEAP!SD cards are a nightmare!Free cloud storage has a BIG catch - there's a record period followed by a "cooldown" so it will record for 10 seconds or so and then there will be a gap for 10 seconds before it will record again. Sloth criminals beware!...If you need a quick cheap cam to monitor something then this would be fine. However, if you have Nest, Ring, or any other service already... DO NOT BOTHER with this. Spend the money and buy a product that will work to actually protect your home!

title: MUST READ THIS
body: I bought 4 Wyze Cameras back in November 2019. Everything including all the functions worked great along with the micro SD cards in the beginning. Roughly 2 months after, the "record event" and the "push notifications" functions stopped working out of no where, at first it was 2-3 cameras and now it's 4 of them, and yes everything was turned on for those 2 functions.I spent more than the past month trouble shooting the cameras, each time the "record event" and the "push notifications" worked 3-5 days and stopped working out of no where. Each time I called or talked to Wyze online support (the online agents were nice and helpful), they gave me all kinds of trouble shooting stuff..... didn't workand the last step they would want me to do is download the "route this" app to test so they can figure out what's going on. Route this app reviews are 1 stars because of the privacy issue, so you get the idea why I didn't do it.I told them that im not downloading the "route this" app and I would not put in anymore effort to trouble shoot it. They agreed to send me an open box camera to test (which got tested by their engineers)..... guess what? the one they sent didn't even work. I reached out to the supervisor that I was talking to about the issue, and he ghosted me. I got fed up and asked if they could replace all of my cameras and test them all they want instead of asking me to do it by emails and phone calls. They ignored my emails and didn't give me straight answers on the phone, but asked me to do that "route this" app test with crappy privacy issue reviewsOverall, price is cheap but won't work long, just spend more money for a decent camera is better

title: Not sure why this has such good reviews
body: Ok, I bought this because someone is continuously letting their dog dog its business on my side yard and I wanted to see who it is. I put this in the window on that side of my house. This happens every day (or night). Looking at the live stream through my phone, the quality is really good. However, the motion detection doesn't work at all. I walked past the window that I have the camera set up several times trying to prompt it, even waving my hands and jumping up and down like a fool, but it did not detect anything. The next morning when I tried to view the SD cards just in case it picked up any motion late at night or early morning, when I believe this is taking place, all I get is a bunch of oriental letters, absolutely no video. So, I tried to format the disk (that came with the product) and get a message that it is "write protected" so it cannot be formatted. If I use the record feature thru the app, it records and saves it to my photos, but if I could catch the person doing this to record them, then I really don't need this camera. This is a terrible purchase for what my needs are. If you buy it to just check in on your baby or your dog, it's good. But it is definitely not good for security, as the motion detection does not work. You would have to catch the person yourself and hit record on the app. It's frustrating because the reviews were what lead me to buy this POS. It is definitely going back. This is the second (and last) Wyze product I purchased and both have been crap. So, if you're buying this to just check in on your house or baby through the app, it works. But if you're buying this as a form of security, I absolutely do NOT recommend it.

title: Do Not Support Wyze By Buying These Cameras! They Pulled A Fast One, A Real Switcheroo!
body: I was a relatively early-adopter of Wyze, and I bought seven of their products. I sold all my Nest and Canary cameras. I told my family, friends, and neighbors about Wyze, encouraging them to buy these cameras. The main reason was that Wyze-- unlike the other camera makers-- wasn't taking advantage of their customers by charging high rates for storage of video footage. The Wyze cams allowed you to record straight to a micoSD card so you didn't need to worry about an expensive monthly or annual subscription.Well, that's all gone with the new Wyze business model. I used to get video clips that showed movement for as long as the movement was taking place. For example, if a delivery van was driving by, stopped at my house, then resumed driving, I would get the whole clip recorded. Now Wyze only allows you to see 12 seconds of the video, unless you pay them a fee. While 12 seconds may sound like a lot, I noticed tonight that it's not enough to see my garage door even fully open.These cameras work fine, but they've now been updated. New models are coming in January 2021. They are finally waterproof and can be used outdoors. The little odd crane base on the old ones is really quite useless, and it makes them difficult to mount on the wall. They should have simply had a 1/4-20 UNC or 3/8-16 UNC thread base so it'd work with a standard camera tripod or wall mount.In any event, I will not be supporting Wyze anymore. They really screwed me over. Don't be fooled by how inexpensive the cameras are. They get you with the monthly fees now. While they might seem cheap at first, when you have 5-7 cameras like me, it starts to get very expensive.

title: Excellent camera, but software lets it down...
body: The hardware is pretty dang good for the price. 1920x1080 good "security camera" quality picture and decent infrared LED illumination for night. Decent mounting system although not very flexible pan or tilt when mounting on a wall, works best either upside down or just sitting on a flat surface. Power cord is just about 2 meters, so not really long enough for most mounting applications but fine if you are setting it on a shelf or something. This is a cool concept, but the software is where it lets you down... hard.Setup was ridiculously easy, in fact, suspiciously easy. Download the app, turn on the camera, app displays a QR code on your smartphone and you place it in front of the camera, connect it to your WiFi, and it does the rest. Negative to this is you must have an internet connection to access it (even when your smartphone is on the same WiFi) and you can't use it in any third party DVR solutions. You are stuck using their app. And while the apps user interface is ok, it is not at all reliable.When you start actually trying to use the features of the app you begin to see why this is so cheap. The biggest issues I've had is the motion detection is unreliable and very inconsistent. Even on the lowest sensitivity setting it will detect a fly that is flying around in front of the lens as an alert and record it, yet half the time when I walk in front of it, it doesn't record an alert. Even more frustrating is when it does actually detect an "alert", it only records for about 12 seconds and then stops before the motion stops. So for instance if you are walking around in front of the camera for 20 seconds, it records a few seconds prior to your being in view of the camera, then another 8-10 seconds, then stops while your still moving around in front of it. This can make it completely useless for any sort of security applications where you may not see the face of an intruder until they get closer to the camera. There are also several instances where it doesn’t detect /record motion at all, particularly when it had recently detected one. So this is certainly not at all reliable for actual security use.If you do want to use it for security, I wouldn't rely on the alerts at all. Install a microSD card and you can then go back through the video to find something. If you are lucky, it will have saved an alert recording and you'll at least have a point in time to go to. However this won't do you any good if the camera gets stolen, which is an easy task considering its easy (non permanent) installation method.Some other small but annoying problems are whenever you hit the landing page in the app that shows a preview of your cameras, it doesn't show a live feed of all cameras, or even auto refresh (heck, even 30 second auto refreshes would be useful). So every time you go to the app it is still an old, stale image. You have to go view the cam in order to get a new preview, and half the time it doesn't even update it so you have to do this multiple times.I did do a firmware update on the cameras, as they were out of date when I got them... but clearly there still needs to be more work on these.Anyway, if you want these just to maybe keep an eye on your pets, or just make sure "did I remember to close the bedroom door?" type of things then it should be just fine for that. But as of right now, the software is not ready for any serious task. Which is a shame because the cameras are quite excellent for the cost, and if the software actually worked well this could be a solid five star buy. And considering all these “selling features" are supposed to work, yet don't, is why I only rate it two stars. Honestly since the usability of these things pretty much relies on the software they are really more of a one star camera, but for the price and the image quality that helps make up for the software shortcomings.All in all it is a cool idea, cool hardware, but the software needs a lot of polishing. I think (and hope) over time it will improve and become a reliable product, but at this point in time it is not.UPDATE 5/25/18 after several software and firmware updates things are still the same. Really no improvements at all except bug fixes. The software and the alert functionality is so useless it is a huge shame because this really has the potential to be a great product.UPDATE 12/28/18 after a recent app update things are worse now. The playback timeline feature that allows you to view all the recordings on the SD card doesn’t work anymore. You can no longer see everything, now you must select a timeframe which every time I try it just says “update failed”. So this is now more useless that it was before.There have been several firmware updates for the cameras and things still haven’t improved.Interestingly enough the devs have put in a disclaimer you must agree too, which basically says not to rely on this camera for any security or “life threatening” situations. Just covering their butts, admitting the software is terrible.Such a shame...

title: It works great, but it also fails at it's primary function
body: Updated: My first review of this was 4 stars. I had to slap it down to 2 because it is not reliable. It goes through setup fine. Maybe will connect, vid quality is darn good. You walk down the hall, pull out your phone and connection is lost. I'm talking 15' away. I realize the connection is made via the WiFi network. My point is that it's not like I'm driving into the woods and can't connect. Literally, within the same office it will stop working. As for whoever said REBOOT is a solution, it's not. It may bring it back, but within a few mins, the connection fails again -- and again and again.Here's my initial review:First of all, to all those 1-star goofballs who are worried about having their precious moments sent to China... get aclue. If you have a problem with technology that you can't trust, then you need to dump Google and whatever smart phone you stare at most of the day. This product offers 14 days of rolling storage in a cloud. You have no idea where the cloud is. You have no idea who has access to the data. 1-star reviews that have the words China and spy in themonly means the review is worth the same rating.Now for my review:I gave them 4 stars because for $25, it's a huge amazing piece of tech. It works. That got them 3 stars. I addedanother because, hopefully, of their direction.Out of the box, it would be a challenge for most. Mine included not a single hint of what to do. I found a Wyze video that said the package was to include a quick-start guide. Mine had nothing be the cam and a power cord.Not a deal breaker.Some other issues... When you are configuring it, you need to tell them which SSID you want to connect to along withthe password. However, it requires an 8 character password. So on my test wifi access point, that's wide-open, it wouldnot work. In my office, it would not work. The password was less than 8 chars. Suggestion to Wyze: Do not imposerestrictions on password length. That's not your job. If I want to have a password of 123 that's my deal. Also, take a lesson from Alexa. When configuring the wifi, just use whatever connection my phone has. That's the concept of why a native phone app exists. Don't make me type stuff that I will give you permission to.After I added a password that conformed to their requirements, the cam fired right up. Quality was decent and the app has way more features than expected. I just expected something that would record stuff. It appears to go way beyond that, but I've not clicked into the inner depths yet.Reason for not clicking: The only place I've been able to view cam data is when I'm in my kitchen with the cam. If Igo anywhere else, the app tells me it's connecting... but that's as far as it goes. Attached is one of the screens thatI get when trying to see video when I'm not in the same location as the cam. Being able to view data only when you are in the same room with the cam ... I don't need a cam for that. I have eyeballs.I thought maybe it's the app. I thought I could do better maybe if I logged into the desktop version. Nope. If youlog into wyze.com, you have a dashboard for buying stuff. Not a single thing that allows you any access to cam data.My biggest WTF moment was that as a USB camera, it failed. I plugged it into a Win10 box and ... nothing. I expecteda modern cam to at least be recognized as a device. I can plug a $5 mouse into Win10 and it knows somebody just entered to room. I expected the same. Sure Win10 sucks the big one, so I tried it on Win7 and a linux box. Same result. It's like you're plugging in a USB cord with nothing attached to it.However, for $25, it works. That got it 3 stars. Seeing all the potential that app shows, they got another one.But now I plan to purchase a plain-jane cam to see how it compares. How that test goes will be the footnote to thisreview.One late thing: Why is it that modern apps and websites put light text on light backgrounds? Here's an idea: Make things easy to read. Imagine if all this copy was cyan. That would be stupid right? Ok, I'm done.

title: Nice Camera, not having PIR motion detection, format sd from app does not use the partition table
body: For the price, the night vision is clear and the picture quality is okay, which can tell most interesting thing. For recognizing a person, it will not give much details.The overall quality is generally okay, but the notification may be miserable. The network connection is not stable due to aggregated network traffic.The technical support is not smart. Regardless what issue you have, as long as the problem is not resolved, Wyze CS will send a standard procedure one after another. They may not read what you say in the email, so you do not get some real help besides the their standard troubleshooting procedures.After intensive testing, determined that the camera has no PIR motion detection, which Wyze CS does not tell.This camera use light sensor and following two charts illustrate how it works.1) If camera and light source are facing the same direction, the motion detection is very poor (even the light is also a motion sensor light), unless the moving object has high light reflection, such as glass, mirror, shiny surface, etc. Camera face direction ===> moving object Light source direction ===>2) It works well if the light source is facing (against) the lens: Camera face direction ===> moving object <=== Light source directionPro:Inexpensive, small, and easy to install.Good image resolution, and Night vision.Sensitive sound and light detection.Con:No PIR motion detection.No notification when camera is sleep or not recording.Cannot look at the video on cloud server is camera is offline.Concentrated data flow (see bandwidth analysis below), that may cause network connection issue.Summary:This is not a PIR motion detection camera. It requires a designated light source for motion detection. Notification method is not good for security purpose.The live stream of the camera shows the bandwidth for SD mode is between 70KBps to 150KBps. The attached graph shows that the uploading bandwidth is nearly 5 Mbps (peak at 60Mbps), and this is probably Wyze support always wants to check your router bandwidth. This uploading bandwidth requirement can be higher than most ISP allowed. Such high bandwidth requirement for uploading data in such long time can disturb or even interrupt the app connection.Since the cam does not constantly send data to the cloud server in this speed, the cam firmware should lower its sending speed by extending the send time (current 20-30 sec) to 1-2 minutes or more.A drawback on this cam is the playback from cloud server. The playback is under live stream, which requires to get connection to the cam. If cam is offline (damaged or lost power by intruders), then there is no way to get any recorded video. The playback should not be under the live stream session. Playback from cloud server should be under event session without a cam connection unless playing from the SD card.Other minor issue:The network connection issue. The notification and sensitivity setting is under live stream (Wyze calls it live feed). When changing these setting, it can cause network disconnected, and live stream stops, thus changing setting fails.Then, the device cannot be reachable, be deleted and re-added. This was caused by staled cloud server connection. Only way to resolve it is turn off both cam and app, then wait for a certain time to allow the stale connection going away (keep connection to the cam will make stale-connection linger).New issue in formatting SD card from wyze app:The wyze app does not read partition table to format SD card. It formats the entire SD.Partition 1TB SD with two partitions: 32GB + unused, and put it in Wyze cam. After the 32GB disk is full, users need to reformat the SD from the app (cam is 1000 miles away), the app will format the entire SD card, not just the 32GB partition. Since cam does not support such large capacity, the SD may or may not work depending on the entire SD capacity.

title: A pretty well designed budget security camera with 3 potentially fatal flaws (2 of which might be fixed via updates)...
body: PROS:- Well designed (the way the base can rotate and raise the cam as well as the magnetic mount are pretty nice design features at this price point)- Motion alerts seem to work well as do the sensitivity settings (haven't tested other alerts (e.g. sound, smoke, etc)).- Pretty good STILL image quality (so long as nothing is moving within the frame, the picture is pretty good)- App is rough but seems to work ok, frequent updates- Inexpensive (at $25 all in direct from the manufacturer this camera is worth a shot for basic camera monitoring)- A week of free cloud storage video alerts (12 second clips)CONS:- FATAL FLAW #1 - Recording of 10 FPS means video is almost useless if the subject is moving fast or the camera is too close to the motion, everything is too blurry to be useful. This is probably a hardware limitation and doubt this can be improved via firmware/software updates. Most competitors cameras are 24-30 FPS, though to be fair they are also much more expensive.- FATAL FLAW #2 - Alert recordings of 12 secs are way too short, especially since the first 6 seconds are pre-alert, so often the recording will show a door opening and it will cut-off just before the subject comes into frame. Hopefully they can fix this in the next firmware/software update, at least make it so the recording starts when the alert is triggered and not 6 seconds prior, this way you'd capture more of the action occurring on those 12 seconds.- FATAL FLAW #3 - After an alert is triggered and finished recording the camera takes a few minutes to re-arm itself, which means you won't see what happens for the next few minutes after the initial 12 seconds recording that set off the alert. This too can hopefully be fixed in future software/firmware updates to allow it to re-arm immediately and continue providing new alerts if the trigger (e.g. motion) continues.- The 2 IR bulbs/lights for night/dark recording aren't enough, they can only reliably illuminate out to maybe 5 feet, anything beyond that is too dark.- There should be a way to activate push notifications only during certain time periods, via either setting an activation schedule certain dates/times, or when the owner is out via geo-fencing (e.g. via IFTTT) or detection of the device on the network (e.g. detecting if the owner's iPhone is connected to the local network). Otherwise I have to remember to activate push notifications manually every time I leave my house. And I'd straight up prefer if the camera didn't record anything AT ALL when I'm at home, since I keep triggering alerts and I don't need recordings of myself walking around my own house all day.- Camera takes too long to adapt to changes in lighting (e.g. if camera's in a dark room and someone open a door from a well lit room)- No option to disable status light (status light obviously is handy for the owner, but I don't want the subject being filmed to know weather the camera is on/off, I'd rather it blend in as much to the background).- App is buggy and rough thought they seem to put out frequent updates- Having 24/7 concurrent local recording to a micro sd card is a very nice feature at this price point, especially since the alerts are so short many end up being useless since they cut-out before the subject is in frame, however, the 32gb size limitation takes away some of the usefulness of this feature since that's only about 2-4 days worth of video depending on the quality you are recording in.I would update my rating by 1 start PER fatal flaw they manage to fix via future firmware/software updates...

title: Good camera, horrible app/software
body: Pros: Video quality is pretty good, set up is pretty straightforward, can store 5+ days of footage if you have/buy an SD card, no ongoing monthly fees.Cons: The included USB power cable is short and will barely reach from an outlet straight up to a ceiling, viewing live feed of multiple cameras is slow and clumsy, viewing recorded video is even worse.I bought one of these cameras about 4 months ago after getting a puppy so I could check on her when I had to leave her home alone. Installing the camera and setting it all up to view footage from my phone was pretty simple and painless. One camera was plenty since she was confined to a small area. It worked pretty well for what I needed, I could check the video easily and have live video and audio to make sure she was okay. At that time I was quite satisfied with my Wyze camera.So, after I started making more space available to her while she was home alone, I decided to get 2 more cameras to cover the additional areas. This is where my experience with Wyze started to sour. The cameras have a magnetic base, and the package includes a steel disk and double sided tape that make mounting it really simple and easy, however the included USB power cable is annoyingly short (if you want to install the cameras at the top of a wall or on the ceiling for a better angle, it'd need an outlet directly below it), and I ended up needing to buy longer cables in order to install them where I wanted. Not a huge deal, and definitely something I'd be willing to overlook, but my opinion of Wyze only got worse from there.Viewing the live feed with multiple cameras is slow and clunky. You can only view one feed at a time, and any time you open a different camera's feed it takes anywhere from several seconds to half a minute to load. This means if you're watching one camera and want to quickly change to another... Well, it's not gonna happen. The software is designed in a way that treats each camera like its own system. If you want it to be more like multiple cameras on the same system, you will be as disappointed as I was.These cameras do store video if you use an external SD card (not included). This stored video can be viewed on your phone from the Wyze app, but if you plan to do so, plan to be disappointed again. The only way to navigate through the stored footage is by swiping the screen. It works great if you want to randomly skip anywhere from 15 seconds to 10 minutes, however if you want to go to a precise time it's unnecessarily frustrating. There's also no option to play the footage slowed down or sped up. Seeing as the app is designed to be used from a phone with a small screen and limited controls this is somewhat understandable, however the baffling thing is that Wyze has NO accessibility from a computer unless you physically remove the SD card from the camera and take it to the computer. To neglect making it available over the internet to a computer when it's available via smartphone app is incredibly lazy and incompetent.Video quality wasn't a huge concern for me, but I did find it to be pretty decent. It's worth noting that higher resolution video consumes more storage space and these cameras are limited to a micro SD card for storage, so if you're expecting to store weeks/months of footage you'll want to record in lower quality anyway. Build quality & craftsmanship of the cameras is decent. Either way, I will likely be replacing them with a better system far before they break.Overall: These cameras could be worse, but they could also be a lot better.

title: Great when they work, but horribly unreliable.
body: I have eleven Wyze Cams, one Wyze Cam Pan, five Wyze door (contact) sensors, and two Wyze movement sensors. The cameras are distributed among four different wireless access points (of different brands) and, unfortunately, it's extremely rare when all of the cameras are functioning at the same time. Obviously, the price can't be beat, however, if you are using these for security, it's not a bad idea to have two cameras per location, and hope that at least one of them is operational.I originally started using them with a Google WiFi Mesh Network. That didn't work out, so I started trying them with different routers and access points. Most of them report a wireless signal strength of 95%, with a couple down to 86%. Nothing matters. All of them fail at various times for unknown reasons. Originally, I would jump through hoops trying to get them back online, but I eventually realized that they will, at some point, fix themselves ... most of the time.I just got back from disconnecting and reconnecting the power for one of them. Didn't fix the problem. As far as I know, the camera will magically start working tomorrow. Two of them are plugged into a WiFi outlet because I got tired of walking to my garage every other day to reset them. I was switching them off and on frequently, but then they magically started staying online.Sometimes, the problem is with the Wyze app. For instance, the camera that I just reset (unsuccessfully) was reported as being offline on the summary screen, but if I tapped on it, I'd get a live view. It was that way for a week until it crapped out earlier today. I have also experienced cameras seemingly offline, but still sending video clips to the online service.When they do work, it's very nice. I like the video clips, the latest ability to recognize people versus just movement, the price, the clarity of the image (very good for the price), the ease of sharing cameras, and the accessories (mention in the next paragraph). I use some of the cameras through TinyCam Pro. In some ways, it's much more reliable than the Wyze app. Then again, there are some cameras that never work. All of the cameras' have up-to-date firmware, by the way. Back to the comparison between TinyCam Pro and the Wyze app, I have TinyCam Pro displaying one driveway camera 24/7. At irregular intervals, the image is lost and automatically returns. In comparison, if I leave the Wyze app running with the same camera displayed, it will inevitably lose the image and never recover.The door and movement sensors have worked without a hitch. They are easy to set up, have a good range, and reliably report when they are triggered. I have used one set of sensors since they were first announced and sent by Wyze. Batteries are doing well so far.I hope that the issues are software related because then I can trust that things will eventually improve. If I was using them because I was curious (e.g., checking out a Squirrel Cam every once in awhile), then I'd probably rate them 4½. But since I wanted to use these for security, it really is unacceptable that I can't consistently rely on them to deliver an image when needed. Hence the 2-star rating.

title: Decent camera ruined by software and network issues
body: These are cheap so I bought some. The cameras capture a decent image, but that's where it ends. The software and cloud features leave MUCH to be desired. I've got a few big issues, which I've talked to support about, and their answers made me feel even worse about the product. Here's the main few, with their responses:- The "cloud" feature can only record 12 second clips, ONLY triggered by motion. There are no other options. It makes the feature useless if it's pointed outside (trees move, a lot). Aside from the 12s limit, there is a 5 minute recharge before it will fire again. Here's how that usually plays out in real video terms: a car pulls into fram, stops, door opens, annnnnd the camera cuts off. I did a little test one day when pulling into the driveway. I got out and walked right up the camera. The resulting video didn't even include me getting out of the car. Good thing I'm not a burglar. I asked on Twitter about the 5 min delay, if it was a hardware limit or cloud or what. They said it had to do with the cloud architecture. Honestly, this alone makes this cloud camera useless (or maybe DANGEROUS?).- The cameras drop offline and never reconnect. Sometimes I noticed three or four days later. There's no notification one of your cameras is offline. It took 5 power cycles to get one back online. I asked on Twitter and they blamed my router (it's a gigabit ASUS router, maybe a few years old, but it SCREAMS on all my other hardware (media server, torrents, etc). The Wyze Cam can't even connect to 5GHZ networks, so I thought it was a bit weak they were blaming my network hardware. They offered to investigate the camera, but at this point I'd already lost faith in them. Feels like another dangerous flaw. When the camera detects it's offline, maybe have it reboot itself?- To get true "timeline" recording (ala Nest), with round-the-clock recording, you've gotta insert a micro SD card. These recordings aren't available in the cloud, so you've gotta be on your home network to see them. So you've gotta pay an extra $20ish or so per camera, and they won't work while vacationing, etc.- The timelapse feature is neat. BUT! To use it, you've gotta set it up ahead of time, manually. I figured you'd be able to view of time lapse of anything it had recording. Nope. On top of that, about half the time after recording (like 6 hours), it says "File not found." I'm guessing the onboard processing power isn't beefy enough to process the larger videos.- They don't manufacture these cameras. They buy them from an overseas supplier and slap their brand/app on top. You can feel these in their answers to support. Instead of "we made this and know it's inner workings" it's more like "we read the docs, here's what they say." Just doesn't feel like a real solution.In conclusion: this feels like a DIY project that got mass appeal through the pricepoint, nothing else. The experience of using them is pretty rough, and you'll be missing lots of recordings. If you ONLY want a live cam you can check on manually, it might work for you (if it can stay connected).

title: Inexpensive, supports MicroSD, clear video, and night vision... unfortunately getting worse...
body: I tried this camera at home before installing them at remote locations for monitoring. While testing in my garage, the firmware improved the camera's connectivity and added person detection.... then after the release of the Wyzecam sensor kit, they added the ability to detect sound as well. These cameras not only record events locally to a microSD card, but also generate a 12-sec video with which the Wyze cloud servers will notify the app on your mobile phone as soon as they can. It is truly amazing what they can do with these little devices... Unfortunately, fast forward to today and now there is seems to be more limited 3rd party channels for acquiring the sensor kits and their newer product lines... and the person detection is again MIA.The above and the lack of remote streaming protocol directly from the camera are my only real complaints. Wyze has recently added the ability to view your cameras by name on FireTV devices and it works reasonably well, even if it is a bit slow to start. Even without this feature, these are still extremely suitable for remote monitoring, especially in a supplemental capacity where you have an alarm and motion sensors. I currently use them in this capacity... as well as in my garage where I can now verify remotely that the garage door is closed or if someone is making noise near my garage. These cameras are sensitive at video and detection, but they do include a means to tune this to your particular needs. Beware of insects at a remote location as if you turn the sensitivity down to stop the notifications, you may also miss motion detection that you desire.At less than 25dollars or less than 50dollars for two, I highly recommend these for supplemental monitoring and alerting... but they fall flat if you want to record continuous video to a computer or NAS-type device, or even to the microSD card... and this is why I recommend them as supplemental unless your needs are even more basic.---Update: There seems to be some limit to the number of daily events that you can view. I have had days with many notifications (>75) between my 8 cameras... with some occurring in the morning, yet they disappear as more notifications occur to the point that I only see that days notifications for the last 4 or 5 hours. This seems to be yet another "fly in the ointment". This basically means that to definitely be able to view all of your notifications for one day that you will need to do it in short order -- if you wait too long, you will not be able to see the earliest notifications. So I am lowering my stars by one... and I hope that Wyze Labs will address some of these issues. (I find it doubtful though, as it seems they are more interested in rolling out new paid services than resolving the other issues I mentioned.) So this is still a reserved recommendation... but not by much (as it is difficult to recommend anything below 2 stars). :(

title: Ehh - okay optics for a cheap camera, could use software help
body: The camera image is okay. Not bad for a $25 camera. But, not as crystal clear as some nicer cameras.The software side needs help. Examples:(1) For some reason, the camera will detect and record motion for a garage door opening 300 feet away up the street, but does not even register a person on the front porch 5 feet from the camera. Although today it has really failed. It missed a firetruck going by (lights and sirens, large truck) and it also missed the mail truck delivering mail.(2) You cannot set a zone for motion detection. The low/medium/high does not seem to actually alter the sensitivity. I had to shut off notifications because it sends a notice about every 5-10 minutes. Tree branches moving, shadows, and traffic -- but not a person on the porch.(3) The camera only records 12 seconds after detecting motion. It has yet to record back-to-back clips when motion persists. For example, a car pulling into the driveway sets off a recording of 12 seconds, but it does not continue to record as the person approaches the front door. Unless someone just appears right in front of the camera, it would not record with them close enough to get a good image (although my camera does not actually seem to detect motion right in front of it so maybe this is a moot complaint).(4) Not only will it not record back-to-back, it seems to wait 5-10 minutes before recording again. This is a problem if someone is approaching the house from a distance and it picks up the motion then checks out for a while. Most burglars do not hang out on the front porch for 5-10 minutes.(5) The app has glitches. Some recordings it makes for motion will mysteriously not play in the app when I try to review the recording. I click on the clip and it opens, but the entire touch screen is unresponsive and the clip will not play.In the end, this is a $25 camera. You probably get about what you paid for, maybe a bit less. I am not sure I will buy more of these cameras unless a software update is made that improves the motion detection and enhances features that make the camera more useable and worthwhile. Right now, it is best for viewing and not actual security alerts. I guess if you want to be able to watch your dog or kids, it would be great. The app works well enough for live viewing. Security wise with motion alerts, it is rough.I bought there as an experiment, fully aware of the fact that a cheap camera was probably not all it was advertised to me. The camera by no means exceeded expectations. Again, I only recommend this if you were trying to monitor a pet or otherwise be able to check in with a live view. This camera misses the entire point of a security camera for when you are away from the house.

title: Almost, but Wyze blew it like other cheap cameras
body: Man, almost had a great product, but per usual the company went south with software and privacy.Camera picture is great for the price. No complaints there. Easy setup as well. But that's not good enough for these cameras to have any real value or function unless you want a nanny cam to watch a live feed on. These are not going to meet your needs if you are looking for security cameras. 12 second clips and once a clip is recorded it doesn't seem to start recording again for like...another five minutes. So I just watched a guy slowly walk onto my property...I can't tell you what happened after that because he was walking slow enough to not make much distance over the course of the 12 seconds it recorded...and then by the time the camera was able to start recording a second clip, he was gone and there was nothing to record. So...unless you are lucky you really aren't going to get any helpful security footage with this. You can semi get around this limitation by storing to a memory card, which allegedly allows up to 1 min recorded clips, however the two cameras I first purchased did not work with memory cards and soon after I was sent a notice from Wyze stating that they had sold a bunch of defective cameras and I to contact them to get replacements. Wyze only initially offered one replacement even though both of my cameras didnt work with the memory cards. The replacement they sent was also defective...the micro usb recepticle would not accept the micro usb cord...so no way to power it. Awesome! So I emailed them and complained some more and they sent me two replacement cameras, which they should of done in the first place. They only did this after engaging in a nonsensical lengthy email exchange and only when I threatened to negative review them up and down the internet did they get the replacement cameras in the mail. I have yet to switch them with the original defective cameras because frankly I'm pretty tired of anything having to do with Wyze considering the amount of time I've had to put into getting non defective cameras. So thumbs down to Wyze as a company as well. Also, the detection settings are terrible.And now I'm reading about the privacy issues with these. Great. Eat your heart out China, my life is pretty boring.Don't waste your money on these could of been great but actually suck cameras. Again, unless you want a nanny cam to watch your kids/pets/elderly on live feed, these are absolutely useless. I hope the negative reviews catch up on here and these are accurately rated for what they are.

title: Does not allow enough adjustments to settings
body: Update: I have decided this setup is nothing more than an occasional review of actions. It is almost worthless as a security system since it is likely to miss much of the action due to the relatively short recording time of 12 seconds AND the long reset time before it can be activated again. (About 5 minutes). So if someone is breaking into your house, you get a 12 second clip every 5 minutes or so. On top of that, the relatively long time it takes for you to receive a notice of activity will mean, whatever or whoever activated the camera will be long gone by the time you get the notice. All of this could be cured if you were allowed to adjust the settings. I have lowered my rating to 2 stars and not 1 since the video quality is good for what you pay. For real security, I would look elsewhere.The 2 biggest issues I have with this camera is that there is no way to adjust the auto record time when set for motion detection and it does not reset quickly enough after a triggered recording has completed.
