Biggest problem I see is, when u loose power it’s all down hill. Even with backup batteries it’s a crap shoot. If your trying to protect your family do it right or go home period.Hey, have had outside under ledge for at least 7 months and it’s still kicking and working well in the elements. Keep it dry and it will serve its purpose.

title: A Good Cloud-Based Security Camera
body: I received the Wyze Cam V2 from a friend for Christmas after a D-link camera I owned bit the dust.Setup was a breeze, everything was included in the box and the instructions were simple. Just put the app on your phone, follow the printed instructions and you're good to go. The USB cable is nice and long, so if you want to mount it higher up the wall you definitely can!I like the sticky metal plate they give you with it to hook it up on your wall without having to damage anything too badly. Cameras that require mounting screws obviously do damage to whatever surface you're attaching them to, and typically 3M stuff just pulls off the wall with a bit of heat or adhesive remover without damaging your paint too much.The picture quality is pretty good, a little bit muddied but clear enough that I could tell what was what for the most part in the daytime, and a little less so with the infrared night-mode on at night. Really, for $25, this is exactly what I anticipated. It is certainly as nice in quality as the D-link camera it is replacing (That cost $90 new!).The sound is pretty iffy, though again, I'm not expecting crystal clear audio for $25. I can sort of talk through it, but I don't really use the feature for more than freaking out my cats and girlfriend from another room. Sometimes it cuts off the beginning and end of sentences, but holding the button down longer sort of alleviates this issue. The biggest problem I had with the sound is that the camera immediately turns the sound on so whomever you're speaking to can talk back, and sometimes it did not work when I was testing it.Now for the bad parts: This is a cloud camera, and there have been questions concerning privacy with it. I would never even think to put this camera anywhere that privacy concerns are even an issue. Still, if you don't like the fact that your messy living room and your lazy brown dog are somewhere on the Internet in China, maybe get a hard-wired real security system without the cloud.Honestly, I don't really care. They can look at my cats, and at me downing an entire large size fries from McDonalds while I binge YouTube videos on my TV. If you work for the government, for a sensitive corporation maybe don't buy this.

title: Awesome little camera at great price point!
body: I originally purchased the Wyze Cam Pan for use in our basement so we can keep an eye on our 3.5 year old when he does downstairs to play for a few minutes without supervision (see separate review on that). I was so impressed by the quality/features per price that I decided to add the Wyze Cam as well to be able to view our dog while we are away (he's crated). For the most part, I've been very pleased (and impressed!)The good: The price point is perfect! These days it's actually cheaper to have a wifi camera (with much better quality) than a baby monitor which is just crazy to me. The Wyze Cam feels solid, has legs that "pop" out of the bottom (and allow you to pivot the camera to get an upwards/downward angle, as needed) and allows you the ability to add a memory card to enable continuous recording.The OK: It's great that you can add a memory card... but I was a little disappointed that the maximum capacity is only 32GB. With the price of memory so low right now, I easily would have upped to a 64GB if the device would allow it. Still, it gives you the option to switch between SD and HD which will allow you to record more footage, if desired. And truth be told... the few days which can be recorded in HD on a 32GB memory card is more than sufficient.The "not so good": the picture quality in daylight is superb. The picture quality at night time leaves a little to be desired. It's probably on point with my baby monitor, and I'm not sure what I was expecting, but it was a bit grainy, even close up. Also I've found that the sound is not always synced with the video which makes it hard to use as a "monitor" and more as security footage. Otherwise, I've been very impressed and happy with the quality/features compared to the low price.Give the above, I think this is an AWESOME little camera for anyone starting out with home automation, security or just adding extra "eyes" around the house. We have Fire TV Sticks on all of our TV's and I was hoping that I could use with my Echo Dot to bring it up on the TV... but that feature isn't enabled in the Alexa skill (yet?). It does work using the FireTV remote, however, which is an added level of convenience. I would definitely recommend this!

title: Almost Great
body: I've been using 7 wyze cams and 4 arlo pros to assist me with catching some mice that got into my house. The wyze cam is surprisingly good for the proce, howcer, the is one large caveat... It has a 5 minute cooldown after it detects a motion event!!Basically, lets say something triggers the camera, like dust flying across the camera. The camera will record 12 seconds and then for the next 4 minutes and 48 seconds if anything happens, the camera will not detect it!!! This is the case even if you use an sd card and do local recording.The only work around is to have it record continuously and add a wyze sense motion sensor and have the sensor trigger the camera as well as the camera detection. This gives you a point of reference for viewing your timeline to see what happened.Also, this camera is NOT for outdoor use. You can likely use it outside with some enclosure but likely after a rain, it will fail. Also it needs to be plugged in, so portability would be an issue. For inddors, there is no problem. If I want some odd spot with no outlet, I use a portable phone charger. You will need maybe 5000mA charger for 12 hours of recording.I think the cameras are great and I actually like them more than the Arlo pro and Alro Pro 2. The video quality and night vision is great. Also, unlike the arlo, you can record continuosly. The arlo is great for outdoors. The big difference however is that with the arlo, it will always detect motion with no cooldown. Yes the arlo is more, but after you add the wyze sense and portable charger, they are comparable (I spent $70 for each arlo pro vs about $45 for wyze with extras).For an indoor cam that is going to be plugged in, a wyze cam + sense is an amazing combo (baby monitor, surveillance, etc...). If you are looking for portability, this is not the best choice. For outdoor use, something like an arlo pro would be a better choice.If wyze figures out how to give us no cool down on detection without needing to upload to the cloud (i.e. a free service) without relying on an extra motion sensor, the wyze would be even better. For now, the closest we have to this is with rtsp firmware.

title: Nice, cheap camera that just works!
body: I have been mulling over the purchase of some higher end cameras for around the house, but decided to pick a couple of these up last minute before a vacation, just to keep an eye on the dogs and dog-sitter. At the pricepoint, I expected a bit of hassle or finickiness with the cameras or app--but both cameras and the app worked flawlessly for me over the 10 days we used them.Setup of each camera took just a couple minutes, and was as easy as could be. Plug them in, fire up the app, press a button to activate setup mode, scan a QR code from my phone, give them a few minutes to update firmware, and you are off and running.Picture quality was good, but not exceptional, in both regular and night vision modes. I could easily make out what was going on across a large room, but you're not going to be making out clear details faces unless the subject is within about 8 feet and moving slowly. Night vision was a little iffy--the IR light had a tendency to illuminate the foreground and wash out the background unless you are careful with placement. For just checking in on a house or room now and again though (and especially considering the price tag), I am completely satisfied on that front.Streaming video through the app was just as painless as with my Nest Hello doorbell, which cost many times more, though zooming in was a little wonky. Seems like you can only pinch-zoom into the far left or far right side, and then you have to pan around. Oh well.The cameras include a 2-week trial of cloud video storage. This only lets you save video from 1 camera per account, and is pretty no-frills, but it seemed to capture video from the first instant of motion detection just fine.I have read that these cameras can be integrated with SmartThings, IFTT, and other home automation services, but have not played around with that much. For critical home security, I would certainly spend a little more for functionality and picture quality, but for the price these cameras will definitely be handy to have around to keep an eye on something or someplace. And I would have a hard time believing you'll find any better value at $20 each.

title: Maybe I can clear something up
body: A lot of misinformation out there on this product. This is a clone of Xiaomi hardware that is purchased and rebranded under three brands, including Wyze and iSmartAlarm (Wyze is less expensive and has better software and service). The Wyze rebrand is very much worth it, as they add much better software and service on top of the generic hardware. Currently I am running three of the v2 stationary (non-panning) cameras for indoor security purposes.Pros:Physically the material craftsmanship is sturdy enoughVideo resolution is sufficientIntuitive iPhone appA lot of control over motion/sound alert sensitivity and other optionsShortcuts can be created to power all cameras on and offFree cloud storage for 12 second alert videos, along with some other limited data (still testing)Cons:Alert videos are only 12 seconds long with no extended option other than 1 minute for local storage, or always on recording (harder on the micro SD cards regardless of if they are high endurance type)If your local storage is damaged/stolen, you only have the 12 second clips to rely uponConnecting to live stream sometimes requires multiple attemptsShortcuts such as Power On and Power Off require up to 30 seconds to process, and the change is not reflected on the camera previews until you individually tap into each camera (at which point it shows the camera is actually off). If you don't tap in, it appears as if the cameras are still turned on. They are not still recording as some reviewers have stated, but I see how they might think that.Since I am using these in personal spaces, when I get home for the day I just put a baseball cap over them since they are on desks/counters, and then I can walk around naked without worry. :) I plan to hide them in half-size tissue boxes.UPDATE:A software update has improved how the apps show the camera power status. It is more clear now and seems to power on and off a bit faster. I like the new features being released.In addition to iOS, I have tested the Android app with complete success. I maintain my "Buy" recommendation.

title: For the price, a very capable, compact camera.  I hold my breath for longevity.
body: My first do-it-yourself camera installation (ROCAM) finally gave up the ghost after 15 months on the job. Although the ability to move the camera angle of view was useful, I found that it wasn't really necessary for me. Therefore, looking for a replacement, a fixed camera would do...a fixed camera that wouldn't cost any more than my ROCAM. So, I took the plunge with one Wyze camera, at $24.99 Prime plus tax, a bargain. I was impressed with the ease of setup, the picture quality and the compactness. I did not want to rely on a sticky ring and magnet base for my attachment, however. So, I bought a $9.99 after market adjustable mount that screws into the wood of my eaves. That said, I temporarily set up my first camera with the provided sticky ring and plate on my old camera mount. I don't know what the sticky ring is made of, but I could not pry it off my old camera mount. The sticky part is a solid attachment. I don't know how secure the magnet attachment would be for strong winds and vibrations from slamming doors, etc. The night vision illumination has been a bit of a disappointment compared to the previous camera. It'll do however, unless I have ninja visitors The back porch camera has difficulty illuminating the brown outdoor carpet. So, I placed little reflectors at various points to give me an outline of the floor area I want monitor at night. It's a moot point now since I added a motion floodlight for that area as well. I do like the fact that short term free cloud storage comes with the cameras. I also have small SD cards in each camera. The ability to pick the area that will trigger a motion alert is helpful. I will see how helpful during an upcoming vacation. I feel confident enough in this camera that I now have three installed outside not exposed to weather. Some jury rigging to allow for the power cable is acceptable to me. Someone else might not like the wire and USB adapter requirement. As long as they work, are relatively unobtrusive and last a couple of years, I'm very happy. We'll see about the lifespan. For now I am a fan, and so is my next door neighbor who followed suit.

title: Really surprised in the performance
body: I bought this at the end of prime day via Alexa for a really great price. Installation was simple, download the app and add the camera, a code shows up on your cell and you just point the camera at the code. I also enabled the wyze skill in Alexa and can show the camera on my echo show as well as my television via the Fire Stick with Alexa remote. The camera can take a 32 GB sd card for local storage as well as stores 12 second clips to the cloud for no cost.Plus - Motion captures via events to the cloud in 12 second clips, to the sd card 1 minute at a time. Motion capture through a window works much better than my other cameras. Playback from the sd card is a sliding timescale independent of the event capture to the cloud. You can turn off the IR lights if you have good lighting, for example a street light. As good or better resolution than my other higher priced camera system. You can turn off status light so no one knows you are recording. Live view can be called up via Alexa on my echo show and my TV via Fire TV stick with alexa.Minus - The free cloud events have a cool down time between captures, unless you opt to pay for storage. Changing an SD card could be a problem depending on where mounted. Scheduling seems to be for cloud based events only. I haven't tried the rule based scheduling yet.Overall – I was really impressed. The playback off the SD card is really nice with the sliding timescale, each motion capture can be quickly identified in the timescale and shown quickly. My other camera just has a backup to a USB drive, and has a promised local storage solution. This one works now, and is very easy to use. It captures motion through a window very well and has the ability to target the motion detection by drawing a box around the area to detect. So if you have a tree in the picture it won't keep firing off clips because a bird landed on a tree. I really prefer this camera to my other more expensive system. Good camera for a tech guy. I will be buying a few more.

title: Wyze Cam V2
body: I know these arent meant to replace a decent surveillance system, but they do ok. I have mine in the upper corner of windows pointing out, so theres some "Scottish Ingenuity" involved (yes, ive seen the "covers" you can get for indoor cams, but not yet for me). but other than the nightime glare issue, they do ok for my current low needsI went ahead and bought SDHC to do the longer saved playbacks.----- Pros -----The Magnet - fairly strong, ty, made odd placements easier.Very adjustable articulating arm, you can get it pointing almost anywhere.The Software- I like the event mgnt up to a point. I'd write it different, but its probably driven by their AWS interface.- havent tried an RTS setup which would 'fix' some of the software issues.- lots of camera options I havent tried yet, might be more specific to 3rd party RTS software.----- Cons -----Power - investigating battery replacement for the typical micro-usb-5V-ac, but running even a small cord up a window frame is a PIA. Oh, but their extension .... (the outdoor cams have batts I think)Detection - still not great, getting better. It is not done at the camera is all I can gather.Night vision - is good inside-only and outside-to-outside, but looking out thru a double pane window theres alot of glare at night - you have to have the inside light off or blocked somehow; i.e. the "Scottish Ingenuity". this with night vision on or off (worse on)The Software.no PC Version ? ("its in the works ..") Really ? dont they think we'd want to watch the output from our desks ? Cludgy on-top-of-ol-android-emulator solution is not for the faint of heart. Luckily I had a fairly recent off-plan droid phone to use for a monitor - use WiFi not Data.The 12 Sec event window - Events are trimmed at 12 seconds and for me at least they seem to be missing either the trigger or the ending.the 12 secs still not expandable afaik, hence buying the sdhc card to scroll back many hours looking for that dang possum stealing the cats food.

title: Great price, but detection features leave something to be desired
body: Great price, great picture quality, really like the app interface, love the 14 days of free storage, but the motion and people detection features are lacking.We bought this camera when a package went missing after it was supposedly delivered to our front door. Lo and behold, the day after we installed this camera, the missing package appeared on our front porch. The only problem: the app for this camera showed a whole bunch of "Motion" events for the day, but not one of the events showed anyone near our door. Recording events are stored online for up to 14 days, but each event's recording is only 12 seconds long and there appears to be a forced delay between each event. Fortunately, I had installed a 16 GB SD card in the camera, so we were able to go back through the day's recordings to try to find out what happened. Unfortunately, that turned out to be quite the tedious process. The app is not well suited for viewing a day's worth of recordings, so I had to pull the SD card out of the camera and download the recordings onto my laptop. Then we found that every minute of video is stored in a separate file on the SD card. It took a bit of Googling to figure out how in the world we could watch a day's worth of video without having to click through every minute of video for the entire day. It turns out the easiest way to view these files in order is to open the directory for a day's recordings in VLC, then increase the play speed to 64x. Once we finally got that going, it only took about 15 minutes of watching the day's video recordings fly by to find one of our neighbors dropping the missing package on our porch. I don't know why, but the camera completely failed to detect the neighbor's motion or his face. The little green squares in the captured video (which indicate areas of motion) were entirely focused on a tree near the edge of the picture.There is a lot to like about this camera, so we're going to stick with it for a while. But the SD card is absolutely necessary. We won't be relying on the motion or face detection.

title: Good Quality Good Price
body: I have two dogs so I bought this to keep an eye on them when I'm not home. I keep them in an 8 panel pet yard instead of crating them. It gives them a little more freedom and eliminates having two giant crates since they are decent sized dogs. One of my dogs is a puppy that does not like being confined in a gated area. She was doing everything she could to figure out how to get out of the pen and was successful on two occasions. Hence the need for the camera. I wanted to catch her in the act so I could correct the behavior without being in the room.Pros-compacttwo way communication, HD, night vision, zooming capabilitiesgreat quality video, especially with good lightingdecent nigh visioneasy to set up and connect to your phoneeasy to control from the appAlexa capabilitiesyou can record or take still photostime lapsedate and time stampedmotion and sound detectionyou can automate the cameras with scheduling, or having one camera activate another camera if you have multiples, shortcut controlssmoke alarm detectioncamera sharingmicro SD card slotmagnetic baseCons-this version is not wireless so you are somewhat limited in placement and will have to hide the wire if you mount it on the wall or just be happy to watch a wire dangling off your wallTop heavy compared to the small base it is attached to so it will have to be mounted if you intend to have the camera extended from the base versus sitting it on a table topyou have to hold the "speak" function when talking instead of just being able to click the microphone and being able to speak continuouslysmall base that has a small magnet on the bottom which isn't very powerful as a magnetic connection. If the entire base was magnetic it would be a lot more secure when mounting magneticallyAll in all I am very pleased with these two cameras and the quality of the video. Next time I will spend a little more money and buy the wireless version.

title: Easy to hide
body: I wrote a longer review, but apparently Amazon didn't like it (I still can't figure out why). So, here's my second review that's short and sweet.The camera isn't perfect:The camera is easy to hide.The video quality overall is very good during the daytime, however it's night vision is really limited unless you supplement with another source of illumination, either IR or visible floodlight. IR will give you a black and white image, while the visible light will provide color.The motion detection doesn't always detect a person. I have one camera that never misses, and a couple others that need to see a dance routine before they trigger. However the camera is continuously recording so if need be, you can go back and manually search. It's a PITA, but it can be done...IF you know when to look.Shadows cast by the sun, or headlights will cause a lot of false alarms.Bug eyeballs illuminated by the IR LED's will also trigger a false alarm as the bugs fly by.Loud noises will also give a lot of false alarms.Overall, I experience probably 90% false alarms. The camera will email those alarms, and won't send another until 5 minutes later. This has caused me to miss some real events, where I then had to manually search the camera's memory to find them.WiFi connections can really be iffy also, depending upon how far apart you have things spaced, or if they're mounted outside thick walls or a metal garage door. That said. I've had problems with the WiFi 25 feet away, and through a glass picture window. Just be aware of your 'dead' zones.As with any camera, it's best to provide a 'hide' that's near eye level to capture the facial features, rather than the top of someones ball cap. If possible place where the subject is forced to walk towards the camera for its initial 15 second trigger.Would I buy another? Yes, I would. Understanding the cameras limitations is key.Hope this helps.

title: Excellent Security Cam Answer
body: I am so excited to see the future of home security getting better for we, the consumer. I've put off this aspect of our home for years because the price has always been ridiculous. I figured our trained German Shepherd and a bunch of Glocks, Beretta's, and HK's were enough! Then a friend turned me on to the Wyze product. For the price and with Amazon's return policy, I figured there was nothing to lose. I ordered 2 and within a few days they were on my doorstep.I had already downloaded the app and created an account before they showed up. After unpacking the cams, I set them up (easy) and had one in my garage within minutes. I got it set where I wanted it to see the garage door, the entry into the house, and the 2 vehicles. I tested it with the lights on, in SD and HD (nice features), night vision (see photo) and tested the sound. Not bad at all!I then set another up over the loft to see the living room. My wife scared the hell out of me the following day as I was lounging and watching a movie, when all of the sudden I hear her "HEY! What are you doing!?". It was the camera and the audio was very clear. I ordered 2 more that day.You can set up the sensitivity for audio and motion detection. I love that my Apple Watch will notify me when I don't have my phone near me. The only gripes I have with Wyze Cam system are as follows:1. 5G Wi-Fi support. Come on guys... it's 2020. Let's get with the times. 2G is slow.2. Notifications are about 30 seconds after an event. If someone came in my house, I would like to know as it was happening so I could get to my guns quicker.3. Obviously people are buying out the black camera... let's keep it in production so it's not always out of stock.I am very impressed with Wyze. The price is right, the cameras perform and the company is committed to update their firmware moving forward. Let's hope it grow and is a serious RING competitor (who are very over priced!!).

title: Hardware is excellent for the price!
body: First of all I have to give credit where credit is due. I have both the Wyze cam and the Wyze pan and zoom cam. The hardware on these cameras is nothing short of amazing for the price. I honestly have better reliability performance from these cameras than I do from my Ring which is roughly 10 times the cost plus a cloud subscription.Pros: Cost obviously. Performance and image quality are amazing. Ease of setup is downright remarkable. Alexa integration is simple and works well with Fire TV. Motion and person detection works very well also.Cons: Motion sensors work well, however... after an event, the camera will record for only 12 seconds. This would be ok with the time stamp and the micro card because you can just go back and watch as much as you need (this feature also works very well) right on your phone. Here’s the bad part. After the event and 12 seconds of recording, the camera will not capture another event for 5 minutes (or the 4 minutes 48 seconds left of the 5 minutes, not sure). A lot can happen in 5 minutes! In comparison with Ring, Ring will record for about a minute but then will trigger another event immediately after it’s done recording so you don’t miss anything. Obviously this is preferable. Even if it would only pick up person detection every time and have a lapse of 5 minutes in other motion that would be better.Altogether for the price, it is an excellent product for live view or micro card recording but some of the detection and motion features leave much to be desired.**Update**Wyze cam continues to innovate and improve on their product continuously! One of these updates elevates the above “con” and offers “complete motion capture” which not only records the compete motion event, it also eliminates the 5 minute wait between events. Right now, they are offering a free 14 day trial of this service and then it will be $1.49 per month for the subscription.

title: Best connected cameras for the price
body: I bought 3 of these from the main website, but I rely on Amazon reviews for feedback, so I wanted to add my own.For the price, there is not much else out there that does everything this can. The engineering is surprisingly thoughtful with the magnetic base and the longer than normal cable.Read up first, this doesn't support 5gz wifi. I have no problems at all on my 2.4ghz network. Know a few things about your network if you want to go to network connected cameras. The routers provided by your ISP are generally terrible and might be the cause of frustration for many saying they couldn't connect correctly. I use my own router that was top of the line 3 years ago and so far I've had no issues.Setup could use better written instructions, but I had no trouble getting my cameras to scan the QRC code and get connected. So far, after a week, I've had no connection issues. Right now you must use their mobile app to view, but it seems they are working on a browser connection with future upgrades.The picture is very solid. It only gives like 10 frames a second at peak, but I didn't expect anything else and the video is able to be watched. The night vision mode is set to auto and works very well at getting a visible view without having to manually adjust anything.The audio is subpar, but I never anticipated using this feature much. That might be important to some, but for me it was a none issue. And these are not completely wireless, you'll either need to supply your own battery pack, or have it near an outlet. They do provide a decently long USB cable, which was a nice touch. These also should not be outside in the elements, they just aren't equipped to deal with those conditions. Indoor only.If you need a few cameras to be able to remote in and check out a location and you understand these have a few limitations, I highly recommend these.

title: UPDATE: 4 stars
body: UPDATE:I'm giving this 4 stars (it was 1 star). After many calls and emails, I was able to reach someone via email who responded and communicated with me until the problem was solved. The camera always worked fine but I was not receiving any alerts. As well as for some reason the camera won't give me pop-ups on my phone (Galaxy s8 ) as it should. (It works for my text mssgs but not for this camera.) However it does give an alert sound (that I chose) as well as a notification (just not a pop-up which I prefer). It has a good motion, sound detection and picture is clear. It only records I think 11 seconds then does not start recording again until 5 seconds. Add sound detection and it'll record within 3 sec. So it's not continuous but it'll work ok for me. I just need to know who isn't respecting my privacy and entering my room. 😲 Not sure I would use it for major security because you might miss something important. Would be 5 stars if I received the pop-ups and if it recorded longer with no pauses or downtime.1st REVIEW:The only problem I have found so far is I don't receive and notifications... and that's a bug problem. You cannot reach anyone at all on the phone and most of the time recordings says call back later and hangs up.I had to email them and someone called me back. WASTE of time He reviewed all the settings, etc., everything was set correctly so his reply was hmmm? He said there must be a bug and he would call me back. He never did. Again emails were sent and I received the same automated written response over and over giving me a huge list steps. These were the same steps the previous rep did, the rep that has now ghosted me.The camera seems good, good picture, I could see the picture live but whos going to keep the app open.I whil return this camera if it is not fixed in 7 more days. I need a functioning camera!UPDATE:

title: Really great for the price!
body: I bought the 2 camera bundle for around $50. I use them in my home to check on my dog while I’m at work.Setting them up was easy, the app prompts you to do each step, and once I had one set up, the second was a breeze.They came with little metal discs and double sided adhesive, you stick the metal disc to the wall and then the cameras have a strong magnet on the little stand they’re attached to and stick to the disc, which means you can rotate and tilt and slide around to get a decent angle.The power cables are just long enough for my use, a little slack but not much. (6 or 8’ cables).The quality is about what I expected, it’s HD but not like dvd HD. Full screen on my phone, it’s a pretty crisp and wide lens. You can use your fingers to zoom in and then it gets a little pixelated. I’m very happy with it for just checking up on my dog. And if I work late, and it’s dark in my apartment, I love the auto switch to night vision (black and white). You still get a lot of clarity.I’ve had them for a couple weeks now and have had zero issues. The only reason I’ve ever not been able to check them is if I’m somewhere in a cellular dead zone.It comes with 2 weeks of Cam+ or whatever which has cloud storage and more features. I haven’t gotten it to record or even trigger an “event” (I wanted it to alert me to noise) to see if my dog triggers it if being loud. Nothing. I may have set it wrong in the app or need to adjust sensitivity. But that’s the only “issue” I’ve had.Would these be good for security? I think so, I think as long as there’s enough light and an appropriate angel, you could identify people with these. Though, this company makes cams for outside use, if you want on your driveway or front door.All in all, I’m satisfied with my purchase, and like checking up on my dog throughout the day.

title: Great Wifi Cam for Most - MAY NOT WORK WITH GOOGLE WIFI - UPDATED COMMENTS
body: I have two Amcrest HD cams at my home. I always have issues with getting them set up, connectivity, or getting notifications. The Wyze cam was so insanely easy to set up and start using. Check Youtube reviews and see how much camera you get for such a low price, PLUS FREE ROLLING 14 DAY STORAGE. This camera can even alert you if your smoke or CO detector goes off in your home. How cool is that?! If you need pan and tilt, Wyze makes one of those too for just a little more. Wyze is a great company that keeps updating its products. Only con I have is they need to include days of the week for scheduling, rather than just hours of the day. I am sure that update is coming.If you have been searching through wifi cams and trying to find a great one at a terrific price, here it is.Also, you can turn these off from the app and they will not keep recording. If you are still paranoid, plug them into an Alexa compatible wall socket and use that to turn it off and on.GOOGLE WIFI ISSUE:Be aware, my son bought two of these cameras (Wyze Cam V2) and cannot use them on his Google wifi since the router uses the same ssid for both 2.4ghz and 5ghz. So you cannot easily set these up on Google wifi because you must set them up on 2.4ghz. If you are able to select your 2.4ghz wifi on your phone, then you will be able to set these up easily. Wyze needs to get these cams up to date with 5ghz compatibility.UPDATE: After hitting a dead end with Wyze support, I found a work around for Google Wifi. I duplicated my son's wifi SSID and his password for my guest network on my router (TP-Link) and only had the 2.4ghz band enabled. I set up one camera using that network at my house and it works perfectly now at his home on Google Wifi. He was even able to add the 2nd Wyze Cam without any issues from the phone app. So, there is a way....

title: Nice inexpensive camera, but a few issues
body: Pretty decent camera for the price. I guess, can't really expect that much from a $20 camera. Image quality is nice.Major drawback I guess is that you really need a memory card with 32GB being the maximum to make it useful as a "security monitoring" camera. I'm getting a memory card to see if this resolves my biggest issues with the camera.The clips are only 12 seconds long from when the motion was detected. That's really not enough time. I wish it was at least 20 seconds. There's a 5 minute cool down between event triggers that isn't configurable. So, you get 12 seconds of video and have no idea if anything is happening for the next 5 minutes. Supposedly, if you have a memory card it'll keep recording as long as there motion. Although there's still the 5 minute cool down. So, if the action stops and the recording stops, if something happens during the 5 minutes, it's not recorded. At lease in the event trigger mode. In continuous recording mode it'll just keep recording of course. Issue with that is maximum memory card size is 32GB which is only about 90 minutes of video according to what I've read.Only other issue is that since this is an indoor only cam, trying to point it through the window at night results in the reflection/glare from the infrared lights in the camera. You can turn those off of course, but then you don't have night vision. So, you have to angle the camera in such a way as not to get any glare or reflection. Of course, doing that isn't always possible if you're wanting to point it at a certain area.Still overall I'm happy with it. Picks up motion pretty well. Hoping the memory card will solve my issue with the recording length. I have turned off the infrared as I'm using this for my walk way and the porch lights being motion triggered provide enough light.

title: Good value, good support, UX needs tweaking
body: [[VIDEOID:1e48048ecf4ae16f4429e17c0fa04ec4]] The WyzeCam is a good cam as long as you know its limitations. The first limitation is the length of recording when motion is detected. Recording is about 15 seconds. I hope recording length could be lengthened via firmware update. 15 seconds isn't long enough if you are going to use it to video porch thieves, or have it outside by a bird feeder recording song birds. The recording I've attached to the review is a recording of live view. When you install the iOS app, and go through the installation, users need to accept all the notification switches because viewing cam footage in the cloud is dependent on accepting notifications. The cam records at 10fps. You can see how an increase up to 30 or more would improve the video. The motion activation works well, It picked up an ant crawling over a rock about a foot away. When you open the iOS app, it opens to a still image. You touch notifications at the bottom of the screen to see if you have any videos based on alerts you have set. To get to the live view, you move the image left by touch. To move back to the notification page you need to touch the arrow on the upper left, you can't swipe left to return to the previous page. UX note: 1. all the functions at the bottom of the initial screen should be on the Live View page. 2. User should be able to disable the mic so that zero audio records. 3. Recording of live view ends when you leave the live view page. It would be better if recording continued until the record button is pressed again. 4. When you delete a video from the Album, the app asks you twice if you are sure, this is a good thing, but if you delete a video from the notification page, it just deletes it directly, without giving you a change to back out of the delete. Notification page deletes should have the same UX as the Album delete.

title: A great little camera for the price.
body: Right off the bat, I own three. Two of which are used outdoors.I got a couple on sale for $20 and one for $25.They are great for the price. Amazing, really.The app is simple to use and the cameras are mainly easy to set up.The picture is really good provided there is a lot of light, like outdoors. If you zoom in, it will get pixelated.Night vision works very well indoors and depending on the location you place it outdoors, it works well (porch) to just alright (looking out over a large yard).Quality has been hit or miss.One camera, outdoors looking over the yard survived a rain storm, no problem. It had no protection, got soaked for days. Is still going strong.The indoor one, however, stopped working correctly after two weeks. It wouldn’t connect to the app. I couldn’t stream. Amazon has provided a replacement so that’s alright. The new works great.For some reason, the cameras are picky about What Micro SD cards you use. It’ll take my Samsung and Sandisk cards but if I plug in my Kingston, the camera malfunctions or something. The app can’t connect to the camera until I remove it. Only one camera worked with it. I made the mistake of removing it and putting it back in. It didn’t work after that. Stick to Samsung or Sandisk or Wyzes own brand.The person detection occasionally mistakes my dog for a person, it’s rare but has happened.Look, for $20-25. It’s an amazing camera. It’s great value. Are there better cameras out there. Yes, but not at this price point. You will have to spend more.For a very low price, you get good picture, great features and peace of mind.I will be buying a couple more cameras from Wyze. Despite a couple quirks, I strongly recommend them.

title: Over-sensitive motion detection makes this less useful
body: Updated Review September 2018: After some back and forth with the folks at Wyze Cam, they diagnosed the problem and ended up swapping out my camera within the warranty period. The new camera works smoothly so far and I'm pretty pleased. A bit of a struggle to get to this point, I think their customer service was overwhelmed. Hopefully this new one (v2, original was v1) doesn't have the same power cycle issues.Updated Review June 2018: I can not recommend this camera to anyone. It constantly tells me to cycle the power with an error code in the app. When it does connect, I have trouble downloading the videos, which fail with a generic message. I've contacted Wyze Labs with multiple support tickets over the past month but have not received a reply or a solution.Original Review: I really want to like this camera—cheap up front cost, no subscription cost—but it has some shortcomings that are hard to overlook. At time of posting, you can only access the camera from the mobile app, but it would be more practical to review security footage from a computer via a web browser. The motion detection is too sensitive, even on the lowest setting, and there doesn't seem to be any way to mask/ignore areas of the screen that are prone to movement. It should be smart enough to know, oh that's a tree branch moving a couple of inches vs oh that's a 6 ft tall man coming to steal my package. When you do have motion detection enabled, you will get a notification every 5 minutes. This 5 minute cooldown on motion sensing means, if the tree branch triggered the motion detection, no other motion will be detected for 5 minutes. There is no way to configure this. I have received a few firmware upgrades since purchasing, so maybe more software improvements are on the way. If it gets better, I'll update my review.

title: Works for baby Cam
body: Our son came to visit for three weeks with their two month old. We needed a baby Cam. I read the specs and thought that I would try this out. I purchased this along with a 10 gb card so we could set up continuous recording. It arrived quickly. Setup was just as the instructions stated and I downloaded their app. I played with the various settings (along with motion and sound) to determine the best for our situation. Because the alerts were coming to my phone every time (each clip is 12 seconds) it wore me out answering and then deleting them (I set this up in a high traffic area in our house to help me adjust settings). Once I figured out that if I set it up for continuous recording I turned the alerts off as we were streaming in the house live. It just worked great for the three weeks they were here. There was about a one second delay but that didn't matter. The livestream of the infant was great in low / no light with the IR turned on. The live stream does not go to their servers but stays local. I tested that by leaving my WiFi router running and unplugging the internet coming in. It still streamed in the house on my local network. I first downloaded and setup the app on an apple I-Pad. It would run from 1 to 3 hours on livestream and then shut off. I went to the WYZE forums and did research. Others had this issue also (need to mention that my I-Pads are very old but work great yet) on certain devices. I switched over to using my old Nexus 6P that last had android updates in 2018 and it worked flawlessly and never shut off. It just streamed. Last item. You can set the picture quality at 360p, Std def, and HD. If the memory gets full on the card it just overwrites. If I recall correctly continuous recording at Std def will last about two days before overwrite.

title: The price is right but its lacking some basic features you can get for free with Alfred
body: The price is right but it's lacking some basic features you can get for free with Alfred. I have 3 old phones with the app Alfred installed. This allows me to use them as security cameras and its free. It stores 1 week for free in the cloud and also has a web page interface as well as an android app to view the live feed and recorded feed. Alfred will continue to record as long as there is motion and will re-activate immediately.Alfred will sense when I leave my house and ask if I would like to activate my cameras.Here are the 2 main problems which if resolved would earn 5 stars:1. Wyze does not have a web interface which is kind of hard to believe. You can use an android emulator app on your pc to view the camera but I don't think most people would be comfortable with finding and installing such an app. Just add the functionality to view on the web!2. Wyze records motion for about 12 seconds. Then it seems to pause or go to sleep and does not record any more motion for what appears to be 5 minutes. So when someone drives up my driveway, it records the vehicle. But then anything they do afterward is not recorded. Example: The mailman drives up my driveway and parks. He does not get recorded exiting his vehicle and placing a package on my doorstep. I have no way of knowing if my package was actually placed at my doorstep. Someone could come up my driveway and steal a package and the only evidence I would have is a video of them approaching my house and no video of them actually stealing an item.Night vision is a problem when placed behind a window. I am assuming the would be the same with any device.If the 2 items previously mentioned were corrected, I would order a few more of these for myself and probably additional units as gifts.

title: Software updates needed to be great!
body: Bought five from wyze directly. Overall for the cost can't be beat. When adding the cost of delivery plus an optional sd card, you can be pushing $40 each which takes a bit away from the low cost appeal.One very annoying feature is the recorded playback is viewed via the app with a drag and drop playback style. I have other cameras and they all have the ability to playback at 2x speed, 4x speed, etc. They even can play reverse at different speeds so you can watch several hours in several minutes. Wyze is only 1x speed with a bar that you drag to a particular time and watch it. If you want to fast forward to an find an event, you have about two hours per inch of payback bar. The effect is very inaccurate and time consuming search of playback. I might not be explaining it correct but to watch playback in 1x time takes up allot of time.The other minor negative is that the free cloud recording of motion or sound alerts are only 10 seconds or so. For free, it's still a nice feature but would be nice to have 30 seconds at least.The wifI only connects via 2.4 GHz wireless, no hardwire via eithernet cord. I'm okay now but I wonder in the future as modems evolve, will these become obsolete? I blame my router but I can't connect to a camera in the garage most of the time.I hope that wyze realizes how annoying the playback is to watch and waste time is able to issue updates via a firmware to make this a great device.PS. Sorry to hear that this is another potential spy device allowed in your home but there are many, starting with your phone. Can't worry about this stuff and my guess is the various agencies don't have time to spy on boring folks. If your up to shady stuff better use postal style mail and completely disconnect from the grid!

title: Wonderful WiFi-connected: cheap price and inventive AI product, if not hard to setup!...
body: Wonderful, unless you consider installation and setup with Alexa (as in EchoShow5) -- which is a nightmare ... !First, I have to admit I don't own a "smart phone" - so my choice for display was Echo Show 5 (sold in bundle).Then I had to download and setup the "Wyze Cam app" - first in Android Google Play, then in Apple Store.Since I don't own either tablet or similar device, I had to borrow those from my daughter.The Google Play version of the Wyze App was impossible! I had a better chance to setup the Wyze App in Apple Store.After I set it up personalized, I had to log in "Wyze Cam" website, to make it work as a camera (Successful Setup!).Then the problems started with "Alexa Integration" with Wyze App "Alexa Skills", a tedious and tiresome work.Eventually I manage to tell Alexa: "Alexa, find my Wyze Camera", and ultimately I saw the camera Video on display!The displayed video in real time on my EchoShow5 is plagued by background noise and echo and delays.The BEST: Cheap, miniature, web support, US support, 14-day video recordings kept in Cloud, up to 32GB micro USB slot as backup. Speaker and microphone - two-way-talk - same as in Ring. Infrared and Motion detection activated.The WORST: Wyze App is hard to come by, difficult to setup, antiquated version of app, needs updating! Also, there is no battery: you need to draw a long power line plugged in (power adapter, cable with USB + micro USB is short) at all times. For Interior use only, camera is not weather/waterproof (There are enclosures available, at your own risk...)OVERALL: Best security camera for the Price, Web and Cloud video backup FREE in segments up to 14 days! Setup is a nightmare, and Wyze app is in dare need of an upgrade!

title: Good and a deal for the price
body: I am nowhere near a technical person. But I know my cameras. I currently have two nest cameras, two eufy cameras and now the Waze camera. Hands sound this is the cheapest most affordable camera with really good picture quality. It's definitely one of the easiest install as well. However you can tell that it is cheap because there are no accessories that came with it. I purchased amount because I needed to mount it to the wall to give me a better range of view. I was tired of people vandalizing my storefront so I got it specifically to look out of my store to see who the heck is damaging my doors and windows. I just installed it today and I'll say I'm very impressed based on paying only $26 for such a good camera. however I am very spoiled with features of the other cameras. The field of view is very small compared to the nest. You can't angle the camera or zoom in and out like on the nest. But then again how often do you do those things? I also like the SD card feature. I have not tried it out but I put an SD card in the camera so we'll see how it works when I do need to review the SD card. I also pay for my nest recordings for 15 days which cost me nearly $150 a year. This device says it will require 15 days at no charge which is a great deal. The eufy is good for places to put the camera where there is no plug in but it is definitely one of the worse cameras I bought since it is not always on to save power since it runs on battery. Personally I would get at least two different cameras just for backup purposes. Based on all the features if you're concerned about cost this is a camera for you. No frills and it works really good with good views. The nest is definitely much more expensive but a better quality camera and sound quality.

title: I would say you get what you pay for. If you don't need a SD Card, Longer Cable, Bigger USB Power
body: There are literally thousands of reviews on this camera and I guess I will add 1 more review to the list.I initially purchased the camera for the feature of person detection for my front door. I wasn't concerned about motion detection. Then Wyze took away the person detection feature and I then had to rely on motion detection which absolutely sucked. Even with motion detection adjusted to the lowest setting the camera would pick up shadows from lights, small insects that were attracted to the camera at night which made the camera useless at night from all the false positive notifications that I finally had to turn the camera off each night which is really pointless to have a video camera when you can only use it during the day. Now it looks like Wyze is bringing back the person detection in beta. Now to the cost of the camera. What most people don't realize or the people reviewing this camera fail to tell you is the camera is just base cost. You will need to buy a SD card in order to record to the camera, without it the 12 second clips are worthless. Then the added cost of a longer USB cable in order to reach an electrical outlet if the included cable happens to be too short. Then add in the cost of a LARGER USB power brick if you plan to daisy chain several cameras and the the added cost of those cables. If you are mounting this camera inside you house close to an outlet then it's a pretty good deal for an inside camera. But if you are installing this camera outside under a eve or walk way to monitor your front door you may have additional costs . So really this is not just a $20 camera and done. In reality a $50 range camera would be a better deal in the long run. Buy Once Cry Once and your needs will be better served.

title: Terrific Value and Performance
body: Why did I buy these cameras? I’m my wife’s primary caregiver and want to be able to see if she’s had a fall. I also want the nanny cam sense of security for times we have outside help care for her. I received the Wyze Camera v2 and the Wyze Pan Cam on time and began using immediately. The set up was easy (and at age 66, I’m not very good with this stuff). The app is easy to use, but don’t be fooled into thinking this inexpensive camera is limited in what it can do. If you drill down through the various sections of the app, you’ll find more sophisticated features you might only expect on more expensive products: Time/Date stamp, photo capture, video capture, Time Lapse, Sensitivitysettings for motion and sound, Smoke and CO2 alarm detection, scheduling and Person Detection (to be replaced by a new partner in 2020), Alexa compatibility and probably some more I haven’t discovered yet.I started with 1 v2 Camera and 1 Pan Cam. I now have 6 & 2 respectively. No problems with any of them.Video quality is good, not excellent, but considering the price the quality is better than I expected - that’s why I purchased 6 more units after on,y a week of testing. Yes, I tested against another popular brand in the same price range. Wyze has the better field of view.. The night vision is good except for one room where I mounted the camera 3 feet from a HVAC vent that blows hot air. This gives the view a heat fogged appearance, but is workable.Would I buy again? I already have. I’ve also purchased 6 Wyze Smart Plugs, a Smart Bulb and plan to purchase at least one of the soon to be released Wyze Lock.P.s. I’ve purchased and tested various brands of Smart Plugs, Bulbs, Motion Sensors and Wyze earned my vote.Buy it!

title: Terrific camera for the price
body: I've had a security camera setup around my place for years, using Blue Iris and three cameras (one on the front door, one on the back door, one in the garage). I love Blue Iris because it has essentially infinite features and configurations, and my two several-year-old Samsung SmartCams have been working great for years. The garage cam, which is off-brand and even older, had finally given up the ghost and needed to be replaced. I looked around at what was available, and this camera is far cheaper than anything else that would work with Blue Iris, provided I was willing to re-flash the firmware with the beta firmware which supports RSTP, necessary for Blue Iris compatibility. Since I'm an Electronics Engineer, I had no problem with that.Long story short, it works great! It's not trivial to upgrade the firmware and get it configured in Blue Iris, but now that it's done, it's detecting motion (I turned off the camera's motion detection and am using the Blue Iris motion detection) and recording videos (again, through Blue Iris, not on the camera) just beautifully.I took off one point because of a personal pet peeve of mine as an EE: The micro USB connector on back of the camera is obviously a surface-mount part rather than a through-hole part, and will rip right off the PCB if you push a little bit too hard -- not even hard, really, even just a bit too firmly. Ask me how I know. I never use surface-mounted connectors when I'm designing PCBs, I always use through-hole parts instead, for exactly that reason. I wound up paying for two cameras just to get one functional one. That being said, it's still about half the cost of just about any other solution, and I still consider it a bargain.

title: You can't beat it for the price but there are better.
body: I have 2 nest cameras, nest doorbell, Samsung camera, D-Link camera, and multiple "spy" cameras. These have decent video quality in good light and acceptable night vision if you're on a budget.The video is below Nest, Samsung, and D-Link standards but better than my micro and pinhole camera quality.Having internal SD card support is great and no subscription is almost too good to be true but it comes at sacrifices. Not all cards are supported and I've had multiple cards that the camera has formatted so zero capacity and then couldn't be reformatted using my computer which makes them useless. The big advantage nest has over these is the preview notifications. I don't have to open the best app every time I get a notification because 99% of the time I see in the notification that it's one of my pets walking around or barking at nothing. With these you have to open the app to see what it was and that process takes way longer than the nest app but it's actually way faster than the D-Link app.I thought mounting was a great idea with the magnetic base until I tried putting it on the side of my fridge to see the garage door. Because the movement is limited, it's actually impossible to manipulate it in that angle.I have every attachment for these 2 cams but they're pretty useless unless you get some better double sided tape or permanently affix them. The tape that's on them is complete junk and won't attach to anything for more than a day.I suggest these for places you want constant recording to a hard copy but isn't a high traffic area. I have one in my garage and one at my desk. For everything else I use nest since I've retired the other units.

title: Good value, has room for improvements
body: This is a good value and good product. I bough 1 first, and it worked well, then I bought 3 more.The camera quality is good, stream to phone well in general. Event clips auto upload to cloud is good.Some observations/feedback to share:* When there are too many motions, the camera only record 12 seconds every 5 minutes. For example if there is a motion at 1:15am that triggered the recording for 12 second, then again, something happened again at 1:18am, it does not create a event for the latter, until it passes 1:20am. I understand there is a cost with cloud storage, and you could use local microSD to complement it. Perhaps offer a premium subscription to allow whoever wants to have events trigger more often, even continues recording to cloud.* It does not handle well when there is a lot of activity going on. One of my cameras is mounting water front to monitor a boat. It could be windy for hours, that generates a lot of ripples and waves on water. When this happens, the video does not come thru to the phone, and the app dies most of the time. Perhaps come up with a higher end model with better processing power?* Spider web problem. I got too many spider web triggered events from the cameras under the roof. Something to consider for product in the future: add a insect repelling sound/wave to the device, so that spiders don't do web there.* The Album menu should be next to the Events menu at the top level, not behind a camera setting menu, because album contain everything from all cameras* When viewing the local SD recording, it's hard to pin point to a time you want to seeHope this helps.

title: Most excellent cam purchase ever!
body: I have to say I was skeptical about a camera in this price range... but I am happy to say that this is one time where the quality and functionality of a product excels far beyond its price point. I have both the Wyze Cam and the Wyze Cam Pan. In fact, I have several now and plan to purchase several more. These cameras are incredible! If you read nothing more than this, I recommend them 100%, love their function and quality, and absolutely will buy more of these Wyze Cams! Worth every dime... and then some!I have used other more expensive cameras and have honestly NOT achieved the same quality of results on many fronts... The Wyze Cam has, thus far, beat them all and is my current favorite! I have the $230 Nest Cam that I use to watch the interior living area of my home... While the Nest cam has a great picture, the Wyze Cam is arguably BETTER! When compared side-by-side, the Wyze Cam is not only clearer and sharper (admittedly by a slim margin), it's more reliable and provides faster frame-rates. My Nest Cam is a great camera, don't get me wrong, but it disconnects often and the video is very often choppy. When I compare the two, I have to honestly say that my Wyze Cam has far les lag, better frame-rates, and remains connected far more consistently. It was also easier to setup than the Nest Cam.The Wyze Cam's audio is fair. The positive is that it is clear and crisp... Compared to my Nest Cam, I would say the Wyze Cam is slightly louder and more clear; Both when transmitting audio and receiving it. It's not perfect. It does drop out a bit at times when communicating, but I've found it easier to use with less issues than my more-expensive Nest camera. Wyze Cam for the win here, but just barely.Night vision quality is surprisingly beautiful! Clear, crisp and bright! No exaggeration. Excellent quality video! Compared to my Nest Cam, they both are about the same... but I think I have to give the edge to Wyze Cam (for sharpness of image and better contrast).I also love that Wyze Cam is compatible to IFTTT, Tiny Cam PRO, and Alexa. My Echo Show, Spot and FireTV devices all pull up the Wyze Cam images quickly.We had been looking at upgrading our fire alarms to smart alarms, but that is an expensive option... Wyze Cam saves the day a bit here because it can listen for both fire alarm and carbon monoxide alarms and then send a push notification to your phone when they go off! Too cool! Yeah, it's not as robust as the full solutions a smart alarm provides but it's completely unexpected from a camera like this and an awesome bonus benefit! Now, even though we don't have smart fire alarms, Wyze Cam is picking up the slack here and giving us a free smart option as a bonus. LOVE IT! It works too!The motion tracking and push notify alerts work well also. Currently, I use the cams for security solutions and to make sure my 85 year old mom with Dementia stays safe at night and during the day. I have the motion tracking set to send me an alert when she's up at night, so that I can make sure she safely gets to the bathroom and back to bed. The sound tracking is also a great feature and has alerted me when mom needed help in her room and I would not have otherwise heard her. My wife and I also use the app in a "baby monitor" function that works perfectly.Using the Tiny Cam PRO app, we can easily pull up ALL of our cameras on the bedroom or living room TV to see what's happening around the house and yard, and even add a little picture-in-picture view on the screen, if we need to watch the front door for the pizza guy while we watch our movie. This is exactly the kind of cool stuff I was hoping a camera solution would provide for us and this one does it and doesn't break the bank.I am so pleased with the options and functionality of this camera. It's hard to believe it costs no more than $30.Are there any negatives? Well, I can't say there's much of anything to complain about at this price point. I have a couple of Zmodo cameras that have decent image quality, but they only work with their proprietary app, they are NOT compatible with Alexa or Tiny Cam PRO and they can't be detected on my router which make it difficult to find work arounds to make them work with other programs, and they have no sound! They also require purchase of a cloud subscription if you want to use them as legitimate security cameras. They also disconnect a lot... I also paid double for them what I paid for the Wyze Cam, so it's a no-brainer... Wyze Cam beats just about every camera I've tried thus far.I do wish they had an outdoor cam, BUT... I've actually been using these outside WITH ZERO ISSUES! I went on Amazon and purchased weatherproof encasements for the cameras, added a dab of silicon to the plugin port on the back to keep the water out, and mounted the cams. They work AWESOME! Super clear images and fantastic night vision quality! I've used both the Wyze Cam and the Wyze Cam Pan OUTSIDE with virtually no problems...Yep, I said virtually no problems... Actually, it's not fair to call them "problems" considering these cameras aren't technically intended for outdoors at all, but I did notice a few things...One, the motion tracking on the Wyze Cam Pan is a bit too sensitive, even when dialed down on the app. It wants to follow everything (i.e. grass moving in the wind, a bug flying by, or when inside it picks up the ceiling fan moving and the dog running around, etc.). You can setup zones for the camera to rotate and auto pan to, which is very cool, but with motion tracking on, it tends to screw up the routine a bit, sometimes leaving the cam to stair at a wall for a bit. These are slight annoyances. On the positive side, the sensitivity of motion tracking is also a plus! It most certainly will catch the slightest movement.... and, when using IFTTT the camera doubles as a motion sensor. Wait... I'm supposed to be talking about negatives.The second negative (slight negative) is the sound of the rotation motor on the Wyze Cam Pan. It's not annoyingly loud in the room, but it can be heard rather loudly when remotely viewing the camera feed or playing back a recording. Not a deal-breaker at all for me... just something that I noticed.Thus far I haven't really noticed any clouding up of the lens or anything like that (when using outdoors), but winter isn't quite yet upon us, so we'll see how it fairs... We have had a couple of rain pours and wind storms and the camera is doing just fine.The night vision LED's are not too noticeable when looking at the camera, but they are very bright towards their intended function; to light up the room or area. They work splendidly! But, I have noticed that if the camera is fixed next to objects that are close in range (especially outside like the overhand of a roof), there tends to be reflection of the light emitting from the IR LEDs in the dark and can tend to wash out the image a bit when night vision is engaged. The picture quality without the night vision is actually very good on its own as long as there are some decent external light sources around, such as porch lights and such. I actually run the Pan camera in the back yard at night without night-vision because the image is usually more visible without the night-vision reflection issues. In the house, however, there are no issues with night vision that I've noticed.We use a Wyze Cam mounted a couple of feet above our front door and that way can watch anyone walking up to the door AND can two-way chat with them!!! Considering alternative options like The Ring cost upwards of $200 and $300, this is just crazy to achieve basically the same thing for $25 bucks!TOTALLY HAPPY with this product and highly recommend it. Yes, there are other cameras that have even better quality image and, yes, you pay for that too. Yes, there are other camera systems that provide a more stable, reliable connection and, again, you're gonna pay for those benefits as well. But, my goodness, when a camera costs only about $25 to $35 bucks with all the amazing features these cameras offer, it's nearly impossible to complain about anything.They enabled me to setup a virtually complete security camera solution for my entire house (as well as a great monitor solution for my elderly parent) and a communication device that enables me to drop-in and chat with anyone in any room of the house (including my pets) while away from home. So cool and I did it all without having to shell out hundreds of dollars, complicated setup and laying cables everywhere.If you're on the fence about whether or not to try one of these cams, just get it! You can't lose for the price! You will be happy... And the quality is nothing to sniff at. These are so surprisingly fantastic that they give my $230 Nest security cam a run for it's money! They work with popular devices like Alexa, IFTTT, TinyCam Pro and the IP addresses are easily view-able from your home network solution so you may be able to get them to work with other apps not mentioned here. These are easy to work with all the way... and the company keeps the firmware updated and running perfectly. I also think they are probably far more secure than a lot of the other Chinese cheapo cams out there that virtually require you to stay connected to a Chinese cloud server somewhere. With the Wyze cam, you can put in an SD card and use it the same as a cloud system, but it's local and secure. They're also based out of Seattle, WA, and the free cloud service option that includes 14 days of limited recording uses Amazon's AWS servers.The app could do with some upgrades. I wish it had different mult-view, maybe some widgets, and I wish it had a better TV-formatted solution, but apps like TinyCam PRO ans Amazon's Alexa and FireTV fills in where the Wyze app lacks a bit for now. It would also be great to select multiple motion detection areas, rather than just one per camera, but it's not a big deal. Like I said, hard to complain when the cost is this affordable. It does a LOT for the money!Thanks Wyze Cam for an awesome product!!!!!!

title: Great little camera that can, and does.
body: Great little camera with just a few caveats. Video is great, sound is great, price is amazing. Software is good, it could be better. I find that the motion detection is a little intense and triggers a bit too often, even at low sensitivity. Saved videos also include sound although I'd rather it didn't and as of right now this cannot be disabled.I love love love that the body is movable and the stand is the way it is, I don't like that it's so loose. I have the camera sitting at one of my entryway windows and the blinds tend to have their way with the camera and cable if I'm not super careful. If the "swivel" were a bit more stiff or could be tightened, this would not be the case.Finally, I absolutely love that there's 14 days of cloud storage included for free. This to me is the driving point of this camera and why it has replaced my Yi Camera 2 as a security cam for my entryway. It also replaced my back yard camera, which was previously a Nest. I tried the Nest as my entryway cam and that only worked with the trial sub I had, it's useless after the sub is up because I only get static pics or can liveview, which isn't great for security. The Yi Camera 2 works very, very well but I found that the clips are short, very short. I suspect it might work better with continuous recording, but I didn't think of that before I switched. The Wyze Cam works perfectly because the clips are longer. Even without the nice feature of human detection, the Wyze Cam is a bit more sensitive at even the lowest setting so it still makes notification of people coming in and going out of the entryway. I can set it to continuously record, which is nice. The Yi may be able to do this, but I need to test it. That was the good. The bad? Well, nothing's perfect. The software as I mentioned, which is still great as compared to others is a bit lacking vs. the Yi software. There's no human detection. You do get an added benefit, the Wyze comes with the ability to trigger if there's a CO or Fire Alarm that goes off, very neat. There's no way to protect the app currently. The Yi and Nest have PIN and fingerprint protection. If you loan someone your phone to play a game or whatever, they can go right into the app and see what videos are there. Finally, not a huge deal but Yi has a more robust variety of ways to access their devices. You have apps from Android and iOS as well as Windows software. Because of the Windows software, you could technically buy a $100 (or way, way less if used) Intel stick or NUC, etc and set it up as sort of a surveillance/cam station with your Yi Cameras. You can depend on the apps when you're out and about or in bed and your station when you're doing some detective-work...there's nothing like seeing crisp 1080p video of your neighbor letting their dog poop in your yard on your 28" screen, or witnessing that UPS or Fedex driver throwing your package across your driveway to your garage door, or, well, you get my drift. You can then take this video and do whatever the heck you need to with it from your PC vs. from an app on your phone. That isn't to say sharing isn't easy from the app because it really is, so don't let NOT having a Windows or Mac app dissuade you, I'm just giving you info/ideas, etc.Nest is now off my list in terms of wifi cams to buy. From now on it's Yi and Wyze, affordable, full featured cameras, not hampered by a cloud sub.Update: A few days later one of the cams has died. Seems a firmware update bricked it. I didn't do anything silly like unplug it and power to my home was not interrupted. I've looked up how to reset it but the directions don't work. Will have to return the camera if reset continues to fail. Color me disappointed. -1 star until they fix fw/software issues.Update 2: A few days later and the second camera no longer 'sees' the microSD. I purchased 2 and will be sending both back at this point. I'm quite disappointed. I guess I'll just stick with my Yi Cameras until I can test the ver. 2 of these cameras.Update 3: Please keep in mind that this is a mini-review for VERSION 2 of the Wyze camera. It seems to be the only camera Wyze is selling along with the Wyze Pan Cam, but just wanted to make it clear that the previous updates (1 and 2) are for the VERSION 1 model camera. Wyze or Amazon have stuffed the reviews all together for some reason, which is why this is an updated vs. new review. Most of the original review works for Version 2.I finally decided to buy version 2 of the cameras for the main reason that they got Echo Show functionality. All of the competing cameras with this functionality cost more money. So far so good. Both survived an initial FW update and both are using microSD cards fine at the moment. As I have learned with these cameras however, time will tell so my expectations are tempered. Loving the Alexa Show integration. It works perfectly, every time. This version of the software also has some little green boxes that pop up around motion detection. This is nice because now your eyes don't have to track all over the video to figure out where motion is in the video or why motion detection was triggered. The only thing lacking for me still (and I'm being greedy here) is human detection which would be so great. Yi cameras also have a nice feature in their software where you set how often you want to be alerted, which is also nice but missing from Wyze software. There's still also no way to lock the software from prying eyes once the phone is unlocked. Again, this isn't a huge deal for me since the cameras are only recording what's going on outside my home vs. inside, but it's a nice little peace-of-mind feature that really should be there.All in all, I just got these cameras and am enjoying them so far. Will see how long these last.I've re-added 3 stars. 1 star off for some missing software features that have been around a very long time in competing camera software. Also changed the title of the review.Update 4 (9/24): So far so good. The cameras have gone through updates and are still working. If you read the review of the previous cameras I purchased, you'll note that they failed after automatic software updates were done. A few features seem to have been added and a few gripes have been alleviated, at least in my case. A trigger for recording sound was added and now, the triggered recording from motion alerts no longer have sound recorded. I have purchased more of these devices, this time to monitor inside my home. Now I can feel better knowing that the recordings won't include sound/conversations etc. I don't want to spy on my family, I just want the video to be there without thinking of it, in case we have an intrusion. I've stopped arming my system daily and am thinking about getting rid of my security system entirely (or opting for something else, anyway). There are also some things I don't remember seeing in the original software with the Wyze Cam v1 that are worth mentioning, like being able to choose where you want motion detection to trigger. I've noticed there's an extra option for full screen or a setting to pick where you want detection to trigger. Seems like Wyze is really listening to its customer base. I'm adding a star due to this.Still wanted/missing: 1. PIN or some sort of app lock. 2. Re-trigger Timer (prevention from triggering again too quickly or, have it trigger more often depending on user preference). 3. Smart Notifications (ie: software decides based on user preference whether to notify more or less often). 4. Smart Motion Detection with preferences like human/animal only or human/animal and vehicle, if smart detection is turned off, then all motion is picked up. 5. Option to end/stop recording as soon as motion stops.Update 5 (8/20/2019): Wyze has spoiled me for pretty much most other cameras at this point. The amazing team has added functionality to the app above and beyond my expectations. Of most importance, I'd say is recently added person detection that actually seems to work very well. It is separated software-wise, so you can specifically see persona detected videos vs. motion detected ones (brilliant!). They've also added a lot of functionality that wasn't there initially like grouping, viewing cameras simultaneously, etc. (too many to list). I can tell you right now that unless you're aiming at say, Netgear's 300+ Arlo Pro 2 or higher, you're not going to find a better solution than these little Wyze cams. I think this will be my last update until Wyze releases a new Wyze cam, as there's not much more I can possibly say. In ending, I highly recommend this product based on quality and value.

title: Great cams for a very affordable price.
body: I replaced my single "Withings Home" cam with 3 of these Wyze cams. My Withings Home, at this time 2 years ago, was $149 and the picture quality as well as reliability remained a struggle. After reading many professional and end user reviews over Wyze, I decided that these cams were a great investment. If they didn't work, I'd simply send them back since my Withings Home still worked. Luckily, so far, no issues.I originally purchased my former Withings Home camera as a way to watch my son sleep in his crib.
